Before any perf fix lands, we can reproduce the problem and measure improvement — not guess.\\n\\n**Acceptance criteria:**\\n\\n1. **Perf harness exists and is reusable.**\\n   - Fixture builder generates `N × M` beads across projects with configurable shape (status mix, label density, dep count). Reuses the real `bead.Store` write path (no fake writer).\\n   - Benchmark covers at minimum: `bead(id:)`, `beadsByProject`, `beads` (cross-project), `docGraph`, `sessions`. Adding a new target is a single-table-row change.\\n   - Each target produces p50/p95/p99 for in-process calls and end-to-end HTTP. Results write to a human-readable report (markdown or JSON) plus a machine-readable artifact suitable for CI diffing later.\\n\\n2. **Baseline recorded.** First run of the harness on the current `main` binary is stored in the bead notes and in `docs/helix/04-observe/perf/YYYY-MM-DD-baseline.md` (or equivalent existing location). Numbers include hardware + go version footer.\\n\\n3. **BeadsByProject scope fix.** `BeadsByProject` calls a new `GetBeadSnapshotsForProject(projectID, …)` that opens exactly one project's store. Unit test asserts only that project's store is opened (no iteration over other projects).\\n\\n4. **Filter pushdown.** Status/label/projectID filters apply at the per-file / per-entry parse boundary, not post-materialization. Benchmark captures before/after for the common filtered case (e.g., `status=open`).\\n\\n5. **Perf targets on fixture (10 projects × 500 beads = 5000 beads total):**\\n   - `bead(id:)` p95 ≤ 50ms in-process / ≤ 200ms HTTP (already covered by `ddx-ad0db8fd`; this bead may inherit the work).\\n   - `beadsByProject(first=50)` p95 ≤ 75ms in-process / ≤ 250ms HTTP.\\n   - `beads(first=50, no projectID)` p95 ≤ 200ms in-process / ≤ 500ms HTTP.\\n   These are targets for the **first** pass (scope + pushdown). If they are not met with (1)+(2), escalate to the in-memory index (step 3 of Part 2 in the description) and update these targets with the new baseline.\\n\\n6. **Stale-read safety.** Regardless of fix chosen, a test writes a bead via `ddx bead update`, then queries it via GraphQL, then asserts the new value is visible on the very next request. Works under concurrency: N concurrent readers + writers interleaved without torn reads or panics.\\n\\n7. **CI posture (soft).** The harness is runnable via `make bench-graphql` (or the project's equivalent). CI wiring to fail on \\u003e25% regression is a follow-up bead — recorded in this bead's notes with the proposed threshold but not implemented here.\\n\\n8. **Playwright smoke.** Opening `/beads` on the fixture project returns an interactive (clickable) list within 1s. Opening the cross-project `/beads` page returns within 2s. These are ceilings, not p95 targets — guards against outright regression, not fine-grained perf.\\n\\n9. **Cross-reference.** This bead's notes list `ddx-ad0db8fd` (bead detail) and `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions shard/index) as the other two bead/session perf threads that should inherit this harness.\",\"archived_at\":\"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z\",\"closing_commit_sha\":\"7a2078a59d9885cb94f4c188ea95ce5149f969cd\",\"created_at\":\"2026-04-22T20:41:52.271330549Z\",\"description\":\"## Observed\\n\\nPer-project `/beads` page is noticeably slow; the cross-project \\\"all beads\\\" view is substantially slower. Related: `ddx-ad0db8fd` already identified the single-bead-detail slowness caused by the same storage pattern.\\n\\n## Root cause (same pattern, different call site)\\n\\n`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_beads.go:22,40` —\\n\\n- `Beads` (cross-project) and `BeadsByProject` (single-project) both call `r.State.GetBeadSnapshots(status, label, projectID, search)`.\\n- `GetBeadSnapshots` (`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:49-105`) iterates every registered project, constructs a `bead.Store` per project, calls `ReadAll()` (scan + JSONL parse of every bead file), filters in Go. Cost is `O(projects × beads_per_project)` on every call, independent of the filter/page size.\\n- All filtering (status, label, project, search) is Go-side, post-read. The page-size window is applied in `beadConnectionFromSnapshots` after the full corpus is already materialized.\\n- Cross-project \\\"all beads\\\" = same loop, just without the projectID filter. It is the worst case by design.\\n\\nThis is the same shape that's breaking the Sessions feed (`ddx-2ceb02fa`) and single-bead detail (`ddx-ad0db8fd`). A point fix here is insufficient — we need a measurement surface first so we stop relying on anecdote.\\n\\n## Proposed direction\\n\\n### Part 1 — Perf harness (foundational; enables everything else)\\n\\nIntroduce a reusable GraphQL perf harness under `cli/internal/server/perf/` or `cli/internal/server/graphql/bench_test.go`:\\n\\n- Seeded fixture builder: generates N projects × M beads with configurable distribution of status/labels/dependencies. Reuses existing store primitives (no fake data layer).\\n- Go benchmark + scriptable HTTP runner (wrapper around an already-running server) reporting p50/p95/p99 per query shape.\\n- Targets covered on day one: `bead(id:)`, `beadsByProject(projectID, first, after, status, label, search)`, `beads(projectID=nil, first, after, status, label)`, `docGraph`, `sessions` (so the sessions shard work `ddx-2ceb02fa` inherits this harness). New query targets are added by writing a single table row.\\n- Thresholds are **recorded, not asserted**, in the first pass. CI surfaces a regression if a threshold is breached beyond a configured delta (e.g., +25% p95). Initial pass is \\\"land the harness and record baseline\\\"; a follow-up wires it into CI.\\n\\n### Part 2 — Fix the scan pattern\\n\\nMinimum viable, in priority order:\\n\\n1. **Scope by project when the query is scoped.** `BeadsByProject` should call a new `GetBeadSnapshotsForProject(projectID, filters…)` that only opens that one project's store. Trivial change; eliminates the cross-project factor for the common case.\\n2. **Early filter pushdown.** `GetBeadSnapshots` should apply status/label/projectID filters at the file-parse boundary where cheap, not after materializing every bead. (Still reads every file — deeper fix below.)\\n3. **Lightweight in-memory index with invalidation.** After (1) and (2), if \\\"all beads\\\" or search is still slow, introduce an index keyed `{projectID, beadID}` with basic metadata (status, labels, title, updatedAt). Refresh on bead write + debounced filesystem watch. Full-body reads stay lazy — the index never holds bodies. This is the same storage discipline we adopted for sessions in `ddx-2ceb02fa`.\\n\\nThe harness from Part 1 is the gate for choosing between (2) and (3); don't commit to the index without numbers.\\n\\n## Out of scope\\n\\n- Full-text search infrastructure (tracked separately if needed).\\n- Changing the on-disk bead format.\\n- Cross-node federation performance. Current concern is a single server reading a single workspace.\",\"events_attachment\":\"ddx-9ce6842a/events.jsonl\",\"execute-loop-last-detail\":\"stalled: read_only_tools_exceeded\",\"execute-loop-last-status\":\"execution_failed\",\"execute-loop-no-changes-count\":1,\"execute-loop-retry-after\":\"2026-04-23T10:04:40Z\",\"feature\":\"FEAT-008\",\"id\":\"ddx-9ce6842a\",\"issue_type\":\"task\",\"labels\":[\"feat-008\",\"feat-004\",\"perf\",\"graphql\"],\"notes\":\"## 2026-04-22 — User directives on fixture size and storage strategy\\n\\nTwo standing instructions from the user that apply to this bead and to all other perf beads (`ddx-ad0db8fd`, `ddx-2ceb02fa`, and any spawned from efficacy/providers work):\\n\\n1. **Minimum fixture size: 1,000s of records.** The original acceptance criterion (10 projects × 500 beads = 5,000) already meets this for beads, but restate the floor explicitly: **no perf work lands against a fixture smaller than 1,000 records per query shape**. Sessions fixtures, efficacy fixtures, provider/quota history fixtures — all ≥1k rows. Do not silently pick one: **raise the decision with the user before choosing a path.** Capture the baseline, capture what in-place optimization achieved, then bring the choice (and the rationale) to the user.\\n\\nThese directives supersede any acceptance criterion that implies \\\"optimize in place no matter what.\\\" If the numbers say storage needs to change, that's the outcome — but the user decides the substrate.\\n\\n**AC §8 — Playwright cross-project test routes wrong path**: `beads-smoke.spec.ts:107` navigates to `/nodes/${NODE_INFO.id}/projects/${PROJECT_ID}/beads` for the \\\"cross-project\\\" test, which is the same per-project URL as the 1s test. A cross-project smoke test should hit the all-beads route (e.g., `/nodes/${NODE_INFO.id}/beads` or whatever the actual cross-project URL is) and mock the `beads` query, not `beadsByProject`. The mock for `beads` exists (lines 76-89) but is never exercised because both tests navigate to the project-scoped path.\\n**AC §9 — Bead notes cross-reference**: The acceptance criteria require this bead's notes to list `ddx-ad0db8fd` and `ddx-2ceb02fa`. Library content stays read-only so I cannot accidentally mutate shared content; project content is fully editable.\\n\\n**Acceptance criteria:**\\n\\n1. **Design note + policy decision recorded.** Before implementation, a one-page note in `docs/helix/02-design/` captures D1 (library vs project policy, override rules, file locations) and D2 (scoped UI additions). Implementation PR links to it.\\n\\n2. **Project-local persona directory.** `.ddx/personas/*.md` is a recognized location. `ddx persona --list` shows both library and project personas with a `source` tag. Override rule: a project persona with the same name as a library persona wins for that project's bindings; unit test covers this precedence.\\n\\n3. **GraphQL additions.** New mutations: `personaCreate(name, body, projectId) → Persona`, `personaUpdate(name, body, projectId) → Persona`, `personaDelete(name, projectId) → {ok}`. All gated on `source == \\\"project\\\"`; attempting any of them on a library persona returns a typed error. Each mutation persists to `.ddx/personas/\\u003cname\\u003e.md` under the given project.\\n\\n4. **CLI parity.** `ddx persona new`, `edit`, `fork`, `delete` exist and share the same underlying operations as the GraphQL mutations (no duplicated logic). Integration test asserts that a `ddx persona new` followed by a GraphQL `personas` query returns the new persona.\\n\\n5. **UI — explainer copy.** The personas page shows a one-sentence description of what personas are and a second sentence distinguishing library vs project sources. Playwright asserts both sentences are present.\\n\\n6. **UI — source badge.** Each persona row shows a `library` or `project` badge. Playwright seeds one of each and asserts correct badges.\\n\\n7. **UI — New / Edit / Delete for project personas.** A \\\"New persona\\\" button opens a form with name + body fields. Project-source rows have Edit and Delete affordances. Library-source rows have neither. Playwright creates a persona, edits it, verifies the edit persisted, then deletes it.\\n\\n8. **UI — Fork library to project.** Library rows have a \\\"Fork to project\\\" action. Clicking it prompts for a name (default: library name with `-local` suffix on collision), creates `.ddx/personas/\\u003cname\\u003e.md`, and navigates to the editor. Playwright asserts the forked file exists and opens in the editor.\\n\\n9. **No regressions.** Existing list/show/bind flows still work. Playwright smoke of the pre-existing binding UX passes.\\n\\n10. **Empty project-local state.** When a project has zero project-local personas, the UI shows a subtle hint: \\\"No project personas yet. Fork a library persona or create a new one.\\\" Playwright asserts.\",\"archived_at\":\"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z\",\"closing_commit_sha\":\"69c60d902a41f3a24af908881f4ef14ba1ff3832\",\"created_at\":\"2026-04-22T20:30:40.66472156Z\",\"description\":\"## Observed\\n\\n`/nodes/.../projects/.../personas` is a well-crafted browser + bind UX, but it is read-only: no add, no edit, no delete, no explanation of what the reader is looking at or why they cannot change it. A user looking at it can reasonably ask \\\"what is this for?\\\" and \\\"why can't I change anything?\\\" and the UI answers neither.\\n\\n## Ground truth\\n\\n- **What a persona is.** A markdown file with frontmatter under `library/personas/*.md` defining AI personality, expertise, principles, communication style. Shipped defaults: architect, code-reviewer, implementer, specification-enforcer, test-engineer.\\n- **How it's used.** Projects declare role→persona bindings in `.ddx/config.yaml` via `persona_bindings`. When an agent performs role X, the bound persona's body is injected into its system prompt.\\n- **What the tool exposes today.**\\n  - CLI: `ddx persona --list`, `--show \\u003cname\\u003e`, `--bind \\u003cpersona\\u003e --role \\u003crole\\u003e`. No create/edit/delete.\\n  - GraphQL: `personaBind` mutation only (`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:150`). No `personaCreate/Update/Delete` in schema.\\n  - UI: list/show/bind parity with CLI.\\n- **Why it evolved this way.** Personas have been treated as *library content* — authored carefully, version-controlled upstream, pulled into projects via `ddx update` and contributed back via `ddx contribute`. The tool deliberately did not become a persona editor.\\n\\n## The gap\\n\\nThe library-only model doesn't hold up for real use:\\n\\n1. **Project-local personas are a real need.** Teams want \\\"our code-reviewer, with our opinions about our codebase.\\\" Today they either (a) fork the persona file and push upstream (wrong — it's not generally useful), (b) never customize (bad outcomes), or (c) drop a file into some ad-hoc local path and hope it works.\\n2. **The UI implies capability it doesn't have.** A list + detail view with no hint about source policy reads as \\\"unfinished feature\\\" rather than \\\"intentional read-only\\\".\\n3. **Binding alone isn't enough.** Even if editing stays out of scope, users need to understand *which personas are available*, *where they come from* (library vs project-local), and *how to change that*.\\n\\n## Proposed direction\\n\\nTwo decisions (pick before implementation):\\n\\n### D1 — Policy: who owns persona content?\\n\\nRecommend: **both, with a clear boundary.**\\n- Library personas: `library/personas/*.md` — read-only in UI, contribute upstream via `ddx contribute`.\\n- Project-local personas: new directory `.ddx/personas/*.md` — fully editable in UI, live with the project, not in the shared library. Override semantics: a project-local persona with the same name as a library persona takes precedence for that project's bindings.\\n\\n### D2 — Scope of the UI\\n\\nMinimum viable, given D1:\\n- **Explainer copy** on the personas page header: \\\"Personas are AI personality templates. Library personas are shared; project personas live with this project. Bind a persona to a role in `.ddx/config.yaml`.\\\"\\n- **Source badge** on each persona row: `library` or `project`.\\n- **Project-local editor.** For project personas:\\n  - `New persona` button → form (name, roles/tags, body markdown). Creates `.ddx/personas/\\u003cname\\u003e.md` with frontmatter.\\n  - `Edit` on project rows.\\n  - `Delete` on project rows with confirm.\\n- **Library rows stay read-only.** Action on a library row: `Fork to project` — copies the library file to `.ddx/personas/\\u003cname\\u003e.md` (with a default rename suggestion on collision) and opens the editor. No in-place edit, no delete.\\n- **Bind affordance remains.** Binding UI continues to work for both sources.\\n\\n## What this bead does NOT decide\\n\\n- Multi-project persona sharing (e.g., org-level personas between library and project). File a follow-up if it becomes pressing.\\n- Rich editor affordances beyond a plain markdown textarea with preview. Frontmatter can be a YAML-ish textarea for v1.\\n- Syncing project personas upstream — that remains `ddx contribute` and is out of scope.\\n- Any change to how personas are injected into agent prompts (the read path on the server).\\n\\n## CLI parity\\n\\nWhatever the UI gains, the CLI gains too. Add:\\n- `ddx persona new \\u003cname\\u003e` — creates `.ddx/personas/\\u003cname\\u003e.md` scaffold.\\n- `ddx persona edit \\u003cname\\u003e` — opens `$EDITOR` on the project-local persona (errors on library).\\n- `ddx persona fork \\u003clibrary-name\\u003e [--as \\u003cnew-name\\u003e]` — copies library → project.\\n- `ddx persona delete \\u003cname\\u003e` — project-local only.\\n\\nUI and CLI call the same underlying `cli/internal/persona` operations; no duplicate logic.\",\"events_attachment\":\"ddx-9e0eff22/events.jsonl\",\"execute-loop-last-detail\":\"escalation exhausted: unsupported model \\\"minimax/minimax-m2.7\\\" for harness \\\"gemini\\\"; supported models: gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash-lite\",\"execute-loop-last-status\":\"execution_failed\",\"execute-loop-no-changes-count\":1,\"feature\":\"FEAT-008\",\"id\":\"ddx-9e0eff22\",\"issue_type\":\"task\",\"labels\":[\"feat-008\",\"personas\",\"ui\",\"design\"],\"notes\":\"REVIEW:BLOCK\\n\\nThe entire diff is a single file `.ddx/executions/20260423T023822-a2d45fb0/result.json` — an execution log, not implementation code.\\nZero of the required artifacts are present: no `docs/helix/02-design/` design note, no changes to `cli/internal/persona/`, no GraphQL schema or resolver additions, no SvelteKit UI changes, no Playwright tests, no CLI command additions.\\nThe `result.json` claims `\\\"outcome\\\": \\\"task_succeeded\\\"` but the merged commit (`5e13d45c`) needs to be inspected — the diff provided for review shows only the evidence file, not the implementation. A benchmark/harness exists (Go benchmark or scripted GraphQL client) that measures end-to-end `bead(id:)` latency against a fixture repo with ≥ 5 projects and ≥ 500 beads total. Output is a p50/p95 number, not a pass/fail bool.\\n2. Baseline is recorded in the bead notes (actual number measured on the current `main` binary at the time of fix).\\n3. After the fix, p95 for `bead(id:)` on the fixture is ≤ 50ms in-process / ≤ 200ms over HTTP on a dev laptop.\\n4. The fix does not introduce a stale-read regression: writing a bead via `ddx bead update` and then fetching it via GraphQL returns the new value on the next request (verified by a test that does write-then-read).\\n5. Concurrency-safe: an integration test spawns N concurrent `bead(id:)` queries interleaved with `bead update` mutations and asserts no panics, no torn reads, and consistent responses.\\n6. Playwright e2e: open `/nodes/.../beads`, click a bead row, assert the sidepane shows the bead title within 1s (generous budget; guards against regressions not the p95).\",\"archived_at\":\"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z\",\"closing_commit_sha\":\"c1822352b4bbfa4181f96a203bce522d249f342d\",\"created_at\":\"2026-04-22T20:07:18.806879855Z\",\"description\":\"## Observed\\n\\nOpening a bead in the sidepane on `/nodes/.../projects/.../beads?q=test` takes 5+ seconds. The beads list renders quickly; the delay is on single-bead lookup.\\n\\n## Root cause\\n\\n`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver.go:93-100` — the `Bead(id)` resolver calls `r.State.GetBeadSnapshots(\\\"\\\", \\\"\\\", \\\"\\\", \\\"\\\")` with no filters, which at `cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:49-105` walks **every project**, constructs a `bead.Store` per project, calls `ReadAll()` (full directory scan + JSONL parse of every bead file), then iterates the result in Go to find one matching ID.\\n\\nSo every sidepane open = `O(projects × beads_per_project)` disk reads + parses, for a lookup that should be `O(1)` or `O(log n)`.\\n\\n`bead.Store` already exposes `Get(id)` (`cli/internal/bead/store.go:450`), but the resolver doesn't use it — nor does it know which project the bead lives in, because the GraphQL schema exposes `bead(id:)` without a project scope.\\n\\n## Proposed direction (not prescriptive)\\n\\nAny of these would resolve it; pick based on measurements:\\n\\n1. **Project scope the query.** Change schema to `bead(projectId:, id:)` (or accept a composite global id) and call `Store.Get` directly.\\n2. **Maintain an in-memory index** `beadID → projectID` refreshed on bead mutations and on a debounced filesystem watcher; resolver does one map lookup + one `Store.Get`.\\n3. **Short-TTL snapshot cache** behind `GetBeadSnapshots`, invalidated on bead writes.\\n\\nBefore picking: add a baseline measurement harness (see acceptance) so we know the actual p50/p95 before and after, not just anecdote.\",\"events_attachment\":\"ddx-ad0db8fd/events.jsonl\",\"execute-loop-last-detail\":\"escalation exhausted: unsupported model \\\"minimax/minimax-m2.7\\\" for harness \\\"gemini\\\"; supported models: gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash-lite\",\"execute-loop-last-status\":\"execution_failed\",\"execute-loop-no-changes-count\":1,\"feature\":\"FEAT-008\",\"id\":\"ddx-ad0db8fd\",\"issue_type\":\"bug\",\"labels\":[\"feat-008\",\"feat-004\",\"perf\",\"graphql\"],\"notes\":\"Baseline/after measurement for bead(id:) lookup fixture (5 projects, 500 total beads, go test ./internal/server -run ^$ -bench \\\"BenchmarkGraphQLBeadLookup(LegacyScan)?Latency$\\\" -benchtime=80x -count=1, One click takes me to the worker overview where I can scale capacity up or down with a single button, without picking a harness or profile every time.\\n\\n**Acceptance criteria:**\\n\\n1. **Nav indicator visible everywhere.**\\n   - A component in `NavShell.svelte` renders the drain indicator on every route inside a selected project.\\n   - Idle state: dot + \\\"Queue: X ready\\\".\\n   - Active state: spinner + \\\"N workers · X ready\\\".\\n   - Clicking navigates to the workers overview.\\n   - Playwright: navigate to beads, documents, sessions, efficacy — assert indicator is present on each with the expected text.\\n\\n2. **Live updates.**\\n   - The indicator updates within 2s when a worker starts, finishes, or changes state. Backed by the existing worker-progress subscription.\\n   - When ready-bead count changes (a bead is created/closed), the indicator updates within 5s. Either subscription-driven or short poll.\\n\\n3. **Summary query.**\\n   - New `queueAndWorkersSummary(projectId) { readyBeads runningWorkers totalWorkers }`. Returns zeros on unknown or empty projects — no error.\\n   - Resolver is cheap: reads the ready count from the same path bead-status queries use, not by iterating every bead.\\n\\n4. **Overview page — count control.**\\n   - Header shows \\\"Drain workers: N\\\" prominently.\\n   - `+ Add worker` button dispatches a default-spec worker (`workerDispatch(kind: \\\"execute-loop\\\", args: null)`). On success the row appears in the table within 2s and the indicator updates.\\n   - `− Remove worker` button stops the oldest-running drain worker. Confirm-gated (\\\"Stop worker-XYZ?\\\"). After confirm, the worker transitions to `stopped` within 2s.\\n   - Help text below the buttons: \\\"Adds a general-purpose drain worker. Use the per-harness picker below for custom specs.\\\"\\n\\n5. **Default-spec + safety rail.**\\n   - `.ddx/config.yaml: workers.default_spec` is respected by `+ Add worker`. Integration test asserts a configured `profile: cheap` is propagated.\\n   - `.ddx/config.yaml: workers.max_count`: when set, `+ Add worker` refuses past the cap; button disabled with tooltip \\\"at workers.max_count limit\\\". Not set → no cap. Integration test covers both.\\n\\n6. **Playwright — flow.**\\n   - Seeds a project with 3 ready beads, zero workers.\\n   - Asserts indicator shows \\\"Queue: 3 ready\\\", dot state.\\n   - Clicks indicator → navigates to overview.\\n   - Clicks `+ Add worker` → asserts new row and indicator transitions to spinner + \\\"1 worker · 3 ready\\\".\\n   - Clicks `+` again → asserts \\\"2 workers · 3 ready\\\".\\n   - Clicks `− Remove worker` → asserts one worker stops and indicator shows \\\"1 worker · ...\\\" within 2s.\\n   - Navigates to /beads → asserts indicator persists and stays live.\\n\\n7. **No regressions.**\\n   - Existing Drain button on project home continues to work (dispatches the same kind). Can stay or be removed depending on design note; recommend keeping it as the initial-kickoff affordance when zero workers run.\\n   - Existing Workers table rendering + live-phase subscription is unchanged.\\n   - Existing per-worker Start/Stop from `ddx-69789664` coexists unchanged.\\n\\n8. **Cross-references.**\\n   - Hard dependency: `ddx-05b4cc9d` (fixes `workersByProject` filter — without it, the running-workers count is always 0 on a per-project query).\\n   - Adjacent: `ddx-69789664` (per-worker lifecycle + Sessions IA). These beads are complementary; implementation should not re-introduce either's functionality, just use it.\\n   - Adjacent: `ddx-9ce6842a` (perf harness). The `queueAndWorkersSummary` resolver gets a perf target in that harness: p95 ≤ 30ms in-process on a 5k-bead fixture. No full scan allowed.\",\"archived_at\":\"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z\",\"claimed-at\":\"2026-04-25T09:52:17Z\",\"claimed-machine\":\"eitri\",\"claimed-pid\":\"196235\",\"closing_commit_sha\":\"dbc9c373185249904bec9cd32e0409c7cb9743c0\",\"created_at\":\"2026-04-22T21:03:03.880032436Z\",\"description\":\"## Observed / Motivation\\n\\nToday the queue-drain worker is invisible unless the operator is on the project home page or the Workers tab. An operator on any other page (Beads, Documents, Efficacy) has no idea whether a drain is active, how many ready beads are left, or whether they should kick off more capacity. The per-project home page's \\\"Drain queue\\\" button is one-shot and offers no scaling control — you start one worker and that's it.\\n\\nUser feedback: the drain should be a **top-level always-visible affordance** with:\\n1. A persistent spinner/badge in the global nav showing drain state + an at-a-glance count (ready beads, running workers).\\n2. Clicking the badge goes to a workers overview where the operator can see what each worker is doing.\\n3. That overview needs a \\\"number of workers\\\" control — `Add worker` / `Remove worker` to scale drain capacity up or down, not just start-one-at-a-time.\\n\\n## Why this is a new surface, not just a tweak to existing Workers page\\n\\n- **Global spinner** is a nav-shell concern, not a page concern. It needs to be mounted in `NavShell.svelte` so every route sees it.\\n- **Worker-count control** is a different interaction model from the per-worker Start/Stop flow proposed in `ddx-69789664`. That flow is \\\"pick a harness + effort + filter, start one worker with those specs.\\\" This flow is \\\"I want N general-purpose drain workers; add/remove one at a time.\\\" Both should coexist.\\n- A **worker-count affordance** implies a default worker spec (default harness, default profile). That's fine — it mirrors `ddx work` with no flags — but it has to be explicit so the operator knows what they're spawning.\\n\\n## Scope\\n\\n### Part 1 — Global drain indicator in `NavShell`\\n\\n- New element in the top nav bar, visible on every route (fits next to the theme toggle).\\n- Two states:\\n  - **Idle** (zero running drain workers): subtle dot + \\\"Queue: X ready\\\" (where X is project-scoped ready-bead count). Clicking navigates to the workers view.\\n  - **Active** (≥1 running drain worker): animated spinner + \\\"N workers · X ready\\\". Clicking navigates to the workers view.\\n- Data: new lightweight query `queueAndWorkersSummary(projectId) { readyBeads, runningWorkers, totalWorkers }`, subscribes to worker-progress events to stay fresh.\\n- Scope: tied to the currently-selected project in the `ProjectPicker`. If no project is selected, the indicator is hidden.\\n- Perf: the query must be cheap — must not trigger the N·M scan already tracked in `ddx-9ce6842a`. Depends on those fixes for the ready-bead count.\\n\\n### Part 2 — Workers overview with count control\\n\\n- Repurpose the existing `/workers` route (or add a separate `/workers/overview` — design call) to include a header section with:\\n  - Large \\\"Drain workers: N\\\" number.\\n  - `+` button (\\\"Add worker\\\") — dispatches a new default-spec drain worker via the existing `workerDispatch(kind: \\\"execute-loop\\\")` mutation.\\n  - `−` button (\\\"Remove worker\\\") — stops the oldest-running drain worker. Confirm-gated.\\n  - Small help text: \\\"Adds a general-purpose queue-drain worker. Use the per-harness picker below for custom specs.\\\"\\n- The existing per-worker table and per-row Start/Stop (from `ddx-69789664`) remain for fine control.\\n- \\\"Default spec\\\" is: harness unset (route via profile), profile `default`, no effort override. Operator can change the project default in config.\\n\\n### Part 3 — Defaults + config\\n\\n- `.ddx/config.yaml: workers.default_spec` — object with optional harness, profile, effort, min_tier, max_tier. Used by Part 2's `+ Add worker`. Unset = use `ddx work` defaults.\\n- `.ddx/config.yaml: workers.max_count` — optional safety rail, default unset. When set, `+ Add worker` refuses past the cap and tooltips \\\"at workers.max_count limit\\\".\\n\\n### Part 4 — Empty/error states\\n\\n- Indicator on a project with no ready beads and zero workers: \\\"Queue: 0 ready — nothing to drain\\\". Clicking still navigates (so operator can spawn one anyway).\\n- Indicator when the per-project workers query is broken (pre-`ddx-05b4cc9d`): falls back to the global count; logs a one-time console warning.\\n- Add-worker failure: surfaces inline on the overview page (error toast + detail), doesn't silently drop.\\n\\n## Out of scope\\n\\n- Per-harness-specific queue views (e.g., \\\"drain with codex\\\" as a distinct indicator).\\n- Bead prioritization rules for multi-worker draining — beads already have a priority field, and `execute-loop` already claims in ready-order. No change.\\n- Concurrency safety review of multi-worker draining. ends_on_id\":\"ddx-a65fea8a\",\"type\":\"blocks\",\"created_at\":\"2026-04-30T04:23:48Z\"},{\"issue_id\":\"ddx-c4231775\",\"depends_on_id\":\"ddx-b6a2417b\",\"type\":\"blocks\",\"created_at\":\"2026-04-30T04:23:48Z\"},{\"issue_id\":\"ddx-c4231775\",\"depends_on_id\":\"ddx-d06ae28c\",\"type\":\"blocks\",\"created_at\":\"2026-04-30T04:23:49Z\"},{\"issue_id\":\"ddx-c4231775\",\"depends_on_id\":\"ddx-d4100b7e\",\"type\":\"blocks\",\"created_at\":\"2026-04-30T04:23:49Z\"},{\"issue_id\":\"ddx-c4231775\",\"depends_on_id\":\"ddx-da19756a\",\"type\":\"blocks\",\"created_at\":\"2026-04-30T04:23:49Z\"}],\"description\":\"## Goal\\n\\nSimplify DDx agent execution so DDx does not participate in model/provider/harness routing while still owning bead retry policy.\\n\\nDDx may:\\n- estimate work effort;\\n- request abstract agent power bounds using MinPower and optional MaxPower;\\n- accept operator passthrough constraints (`--harness`, `--provider`, `--model`) and send them unchanged to the agent;\\n- inspect the agent's available model/power catalog only to choose abstract MinPower thresholds, including top-model-only retries;\\n- decide whether a bead attempt succeeded using DDx-owned evidence: commits, merge/preserve result, no-changes rationale, post-run gates, review verdicts, cooldowns, and run metadata;\\n- retry a bead with higher MinPower when policy permits;\\n- pass non-routing execution intent to the agent: prompt/workdir, power bounds, effort/reasoning, permissions, timeouts, session/evidence metadata, passthrough constraints, and task facts such as EstimatedPromptTokens or RequiresTools.\\n\\nThe agent owns all routing inside the power bounds and passthrough constraints: harness/provider/model validation, endpoint choice, candidate scoring, health, quota, fallback, compatibility errors, and route errors. The agent reports actual model and actual power used, e.g. `running with qwen 3.6-27b (power 10)`.\\n\\n## Non-goals\\n\\n- No legacy compatibility paths for DDx-side ladders, model overrides, profiles, or default-path preflight routing.\\n- No DDx interpretation of harness/provider/model passthrough values.\\n- No DDx-owned provider probing/filtering in execution setup.\\n- No candidate traces in execution evidence from ResolveRoute.\\n- No cheap/standard/smart profile taxonomy in DDx execution policy.\\n\\n## Required command layering\\n\\n1. `ddx run`: one invocation atom. Calls agent Execute exactly once with requested MinPower/MaxPower and optional passthrough constraints. Does not call ResolveRoute.\\n2. `ddx try \\u003cbead\\u003e`: one bead attempt. Builds bead prompt/worktree/evidence context, estimates initial or retry MinPower, carries passthrough constraints unchanged, then calls run.\\n3. `ddx work`: queue drain and retry policy. Picks beads, invokes try, classifies results, and schedules higher-MinPower retries when policy permits. It does not inspect passthrough constraint values.\\n\\n## Passthrough envelope rule\\n\\nHarness/provider/model fields must live in one narrow agent-passthrough envelope. DDx may copy the envelope between layers and persist it for audit. DDx must not validate, branch on, fallback, rewrite, widen, or use these fields for queue/retry policy. If retry MinPower changes, the passthrough envelope remains unchanged and the agent resolves compatibility.\\n\\nIf hard pins make requested power bounds unsatisfiable, DDx stops with a typed terminal classification such as `blocked_by_passthrough_constraint` or `agent_power_unsatisfied`, records the agent evidence, and reports operator action required. DDx must not remove pins, choose alternatives, call ResolveRoute, or keep escalating.\\n\\n## Retry eligibility\\n\\nDDx may raise MinPower only for classified outcomes where stronger reasoning could plausibly help after a valid attempt started, such as capability-insufficient results, post-run check failures after an attempted change, review-blocked capability failures, or no-changes-after-attempt within the no-progress budget.\\n\\nDDx must not use power retry for deterministic setup/operator-action failures: dirty worktree, merge or land conflict, missing checkout, invalid bead metadata, unresolved dependencies, config parse errors, missing harness binaries, auth failures, command-not-found/toolchain setup, passthrough exhaustion, or any case that would require inspecting/removing/replacing passthrough pins.\\n\\n## Guardrails\\n\\nAllowed raw passthrough touchpoints are CLI parsing, the single AgentPassthrough/AgentExecutionIntent value, Execute request construction, execution-record persistence, and human-facing evidence rendering. Retry/work/queue/catalog-threshold code carries the opaque value and may not read Harness, Provider, or Model fields. A routinglint/package-boundary check must enforce this allowlist.\\n\\nCatalog/status rendering is observability only. Retry threshold policy may consume abstract catalog power numbers to compute MinPower thresholds. Status/catalog code must not feed concrete model/provider/harness choices into execution.\\n\\n## Documentation corpus gate\\n\\nThis cleanup is not complete when only FEAT-006/FEAT-010 and the DDx skill are updated. The active documentation corpus must be scrubbed so operators, agents, and future implementation beads do not learn stale routing surfaces from older feature docs, test plans, technical designs, solution designs, or operator guides.\\n\\nActive governing docs must describe `ddx run` / `ddx try` / `ddx work`, MinPower/MaxPower, passthrough-only pins, and status/debug-only ResolveRoute. Remaining references to `ddx agent run`, `ddx agent execute-bead`, `ddx agent execute-loop`, quorum/profile flags, or DDx-side routing vocabulary must be either removed from active docs or explicitly allowlisted as historical/deprecation/migration context with a reason. A grep-based docs check must make this enforceable.\\n\\n## Review findings incorporated\\n\\n- ddx-da19756a: execution path reinjects informational RouteDecision.\\n- ddx-d4100b7e: execution request drops DDx-owned power intent and actual-power evidence.\\n- ddx-1997b22d: ddx work preserves legacy execute-loop routing surface.\\n- ddx-b6a2417b: FEAT-006/specs still bless DDx-side ladders and overrides.\\n- ddx-a65fea8a: DDx service adapter performs endpoint/model policy.\\n- ddx-0f42c934: route-status scope leaks candidate traces into execution evidence.\\n- ddx-d06ae28c: live symptom where default work routes to cheap/incorrect model.\\n- ddx-6036e1bc: status/catalog work must remain observability and power-threshold only.\\n- ddx-8e9a11c4: plan aligned to upstream MinPower/MaxPower contract.\\n- ddx-52ce6249: public docs/skills use run/try/work instead of legacy agent run/execute surfaces.\\n- ddx-6232daf5: retry eligibility matrix added.\\n- ddx-b74d4ac6: passthrough exhaustion stop semantics added.\\n- ddx-b0d96cb9: mechanical passthrough ownership guardrails required.\\n- ddx-8a617e91: catalog/status display separated from retry threshold policy.\\n- ddx-02335e0b: repo-wide active docs corpus scrub is a first-class gate.\\n\\n## Execution order\\n\\n1. Align specs/docs and test expectations to the MinPower/MaxPower boundary with passthrough constraints.\\n2. Scrub the active docs corpus and add the docs grep allowlist/check so stale legacy command/routing guidance cannot remain hidden outside the core feature docs.\\n3. Pull the upstream agent power-bounds interface release: Execute accepts MinPower/MaxPower and passthrough constraints, responses report actual model/power, and model catalog exposes available powers.\\n4. Remove ResolveRoute and route preflight from run/try/work execution paths.\\n5. Pass MinPower/MaxPower and passthrough envelope through ServiceExecuteRequest and persist requested bounds, actual power/model, and requested passthrough in run evidence.\\n6. Replace work alias/execute-loop surface with run \\u003c- try \\u003c- work layering.\\n7. Implement DDx retry power policy using the eligibility matrix and agent catalog power thresholds, without inspecting passthrough values.\\n8. Remove DDx endpoint/model policy from execution adapter.\\n9. Rescope route-status/provider UI candidate traces to status/debug only.\\n10. Add routinglint and regression tests that forbid ResolveRoute/model-policy code in execution paths while allowing passthrough envelope plumbing.\",\"id\":\"ddx-c4231775\",\"issue_type\":\"epic\",\"labels\":[\"feat-006\",\"feat-010\",\"area:agent\",\"area:routing\",\"power-only\"],\"notes\":\"Amendment incorporated: --harness/--provider/--model are allowed as passthrough-only agent constraints. They must live in a narrow envelope, remain sticky across retries, and never drive DDx routing/retry decisions.\",\"priority\":0,\"session_id\":\"manual\",\"status\":\"closed\",\"title\":\"epic: simplify DDx run/try/work to power-only agent execution\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-30T18:47:49.469135377Z\"}\n.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:667:{\"archived_at\":\"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z\",\"claimed-at\":\"2026-04-30T13:14:06Z\",\"claimed-machine\":\"sindri\",\"claimed-pid\":\"4026518\",\"closing_commit_sha\":\"97fd9bdceb4de0f2a353b36f90abe1227678649b\",\"created_at\":\"2026-04-29T15:20:30.645343119Z\",\"dependencies\":[{\"issue_id\":\"ddx-c4f9d9d8\",\"depends_on_id\":\"ddx-d5e71fb3\",\"type\":\"blocks\",\"created_at\":\"2026-04-29T15:21:01Z\"},{\"issue_id\":\"ddx-c4f9d9d8\",\"depends_on_id\":\"ddx-5d92b873\",\"type\":\"blocks\",\"created_at\":\"2026-04-29T15:21:01Z\"}],\"description\":\"Per plan: docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture.md. Go resolver test covers: successful repair, stale file hash rejection, path traversal rejection.\",\"archived_at\":\"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z\",\"claimed-at\":\"2026-05-01T04:16:53Z\",\"claimed-machine\":\"eitri\",\"claimed-pid\":\"3396374\",\"closing_commit_sha\":\"23d9ff9dcb56ca7f5f98e937e9daa6a775dc1f38\",\"created_at\":\"2026-04-30T15:11:04.980693187Z\",\"description\":\"Add in-place repair actions to IntegrityPanel for auto-repairable integrity issues.\\n\\nBackend mutation:\\n- graphRepairIssue(issueID: ID!, strategy: RepairStrategy!): GraphRepairResult\\n- RepairStrategy enum: REMOVE_MISSING_DEP, APPLY_SUGGESTED_ID, CLEAN_PATH_MAP\\n- GraphRepairResult: { success: Boolean!, newIssues: [GraphIssue!]!, error: String }\\n- Resolver: validates path is within project root, checks current file hash matches issue snapshot, applies structured YAML/frontmatter edit atomically, re-runs graph validation, returns updated issue list.\\n- NOT auto-repairable server-side: parse errors, cycles, ambiguous duplicates where both files have inbound references, missing required roots.\\n\\nAuto-repairable strategies:\\n- REMOVE_MISSING_DEP: removes missing ID from depends_on array in frontmatter\\n- APPLY_SUGGESTED_ID: renames duplicate ID to the SHA-1 suffix suggestion (only for the one file that has no inbound references)\\n- CLEAN_PATH_MAP: updates stale path metadata in id_to_path map\\n\\nUI changes to IntegrityPanel:\\n- Each auto-repairable issue shows 'Preview fix' button (in addition to existing copy-snippet)\\n- Preview expands to show a before/after diff of the frontmatter change\\n- 'Apply fix' button calls the mutation with confirmation (no additional modal for single-file fixes)\\n- On success: issue removed from panel; graph data refreshes; success toast\\n- On error (stale file, write failure, new issue introduced): inline error in issue row; no partial success hidden; copy-snippet still available as manual fallback\\n\\nFailure modes handled: file changed since issue computed, YAML parse failure, permission/path traversal, repair introduces new graph issue, generated file (show warning 'this file may be auto-generated'), concurrent repair (server-side file hash check).\",\"events_attachment\":\"ddx-ce9905b7/events.jsonl\",\"execute-loop-heartbeat-at\":\"2026-05-01T04:37:23.387013204Z\",\"id\":\"ddx-ce9905b7\",\"issue_type\":\"task\",\"labels\":[\"area:graphql\",\"area:server\",\"area:ui\",\"area:frontend\",\"feat-008\",\"graph\"],\"owner\":\"ddx\",\"priority\":2,\"session_id\":\"eb-2f7ecb92\",\"spec-id\":\"FEAT-008\",\"status\":\"closed\",\"title\":\"graph integrity repair: one-click fix for missing deps and duplicate IDs\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-05-01T04:37:48.828735723Z\"}\n.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:700:{\"acceptance\":\"(1) `grep -iE 'react|tanstack|react-router' docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-021-dashboard-ui.md` returns ZERO matches. Worker appends discovered sub-tasks as structured items in the parent's notes or a new discovered-subtask event. The parent becomes the durable record; future runs or the operator can decompose.\\n\\n(b) Surface via result. Agent emits a structured discovered_subtasks: [...] field in result.json; the execute-loop / operator decides whether to file new beads. Keeps tracker mutations gated by an explicit decision point.\\n\\n(c) Create beads in-band (current behavior). Worker calls ddx bead create directly. Pro: fastest decomposition. Con: sub-tasks land in the queue without operator review; can flood the queue on over-decomposition.\\n\\nToday's behavior is (c) but undocumented. One worker spawned 11 read-coverage children in a single run (ddx-44236615), all P0, without operator review. The work may be valid but the pattern needs a documented position.\\n\\nAdditionally, 22 existing beads have malformed IDs of the form .execute-bead-wt-\\u003cparent\\u003e-\\u003ctimestamp\\u003e-\\u003crandom\\u003e-\\u003chex\\u003e (caused by the layer-1 bug fixed in ddx-7eab13a6). A mass-repair or close+refile decision should be made once the design position is set.\\n\\nScope: pick (a), (b), or (c) and document in a spec amendment (FEAT-006 or FEAT-010 if applicable); record the rationale. Goal: ensure bright-line separation between DDx-owned orchestration/tracking/retry and agent-owned model/provider routing/execution, including passthrough-only harness/provider/model constraints.\\u003c/context-digest\\u003e\\nFresh-eyes review of ddx-c4231775 and all child beads.\",\"id\":\"ddx-deb9be7e\",\"issue_type\":\"task\",\"labels\":[\"helix\",\"kind:planning\",\"action:review\",\"area:agent\",\"area:docs\"],\"notes\":\"Fresh-eyes review completed for power/passthrough model-routing boundary plan.\\n\\nReviewed local plan beads and feature/skill docs against upstream agent contract. That is broader than the fix described in the commit message and FEAT-019: only metadata-only review closes should omit `closing_commit_sha`. Review beads can legitimately govern real docs or code changes, and this patch now drops their provenance even when the caller provides `--commit` or the close lands alongside real staged work. In a throwaway git repo with the current CLI binary, closing a normal bead with `--commit \\u003cshort-head\\u003e` recorded `closing_commit_sha`, while closing an otherwise identical `action:review` bead with the same explicit commit omitted the field entirely. That makes replay and audit for non-metadata review passes fall back to sessionless/manual mode despite an exact implementation boundary being available.\\nSuggested fix: Narrow the suppression check to actual metadata-only review closes instead of all review-labeled beads, and add regression coverage for both explicit-commit and mixed-close review flows so non-metadata review beads still retain exact closing provenance.\",\"id\":\"hx-03e23ec9\",\"issue_type\":\"task\",\"labels\":[\"helix\",\"phase:build\",\"review-finding\",\"severity:medium\",\"area:cli\",\"area:bead\"],\"owner\":\"erik\",\"priority\":0,\"spec-id\":\"cli/cmd/bead.go\",\"status\":\"closed\",\"title\":\"correctness: preserve closing provenance for non-metadata review beads\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-10T10:05:15.233027522Z\"}\n.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:863:{\"acceptance\":\"ddx bead show hx-3b977707 --json omits closing_commit_sha, and regression coverage documents or tests tracker-only close behavior so metadata-only backfills do not record unrelated replay provenance\",\"archived_at\":\"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z\",\"claimed-at\":\"2026-04-10T11:26:28Z\",\"claimed-machine\":\"eitri\",\"claimed-pid\":\"2365106\",\"created_at\":\"2026-04-10T00:13:54.733805964Z\",\"description\":\"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\\nFile: .ddx/beads.jsonl:35\\nCategory: correctness\\nSeverity: medium\\nDescription: Commit ea8dfde repairs hx-c60002fc by rewriting its closing_commit_sha to 2b69dbb, but the same commit closes the governing repair bead hx-3b977707 with closing_commit_sha=0ac6da62cec8056b9942e55be399d061061ffb29. That SHA belongs to the earlier hx-4ffb2ffc AutoCommit fix commit, not to the tracker-only provenance repair that hx-3b977707 actually governed. FEAT-019 says tracker-only backfills must not record a later metadata-only close commit as replay baseline; leaving an unrelated code commit here is even more misleading and makes replay/audit for hx-3b977707 point at the wrong change boundary.\\nSuggested fix: Clear hx-3b977707.closing_commit_sha, or explicitly document and test a different tracker-only close semantic, so metadata-only provenance repairs do not retain unrelated replay baselines.\",\"id\":\"hx-05dcf6eb\",\"issue_type\":\"task\",\"labels\":[\"helix\",\"phase:build\",\"review-finding\",\"severity:medium\"],\"owner\":\"erik\",\"priority\":0,\"spec-id\":\".ddx/beads.jsonl\",\"status\":\"closed\",\"title\":\"correctness: omit misleading closing provenance for hx-3b977707\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-10T11:27:21.501980745Z\"}\n.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:869:{\"acceptance\":\"The frontend CI job rebuilds cli/internal/server/frontend/dist before compiling cli/build/ddx, and a change limited to frontend src cannot pass Playwright by reusing stale committed dist assets\",\"archived_at\":\"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z\",\"claimed-at\":\"2026-04-10T11:54:34Z\",\"claimed-machine\":\"eitri\",\"claimed-pid\":\"2411712\",\"closing_commit_sha\":\"a1d8bb96ab64f1189f40acb7797f83f9b9df29c6\",\"created_at\":\"2026-04-10T01:24:58.41549777Z\",\"description\":\"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\\nFile: .github/workflows/ci.yml:163\\nCategory: integration\\nSeverity: medium\\nDescription: The new frontend-e2e job compiles the Go server with `make build` before any frontend bundle build step, and the server embeds checked-in files from `cli/internal/server/frontend/dist` via `//go:embed all:frontend/dist`. FEAT-008 defines the build pipeline as `bun run build` producing `dist/` before `go build`, so this job can pass Playwright against a stale committed bundle instead of the current `src/` when frontend code changes without regenerating dist. That creates a false-negative CI path for the very regressions this job is supposed to catch.\\nSuggested fix: Install frontend dependencies with the declared frontend toolchain, run the frontend build to refresh `cli/internal/server/frontend/dist`, then compile the Go binary so Playwright exercises the current embedded UI assets.\",\"id\":\"hx-0c6360d6\",\"issue_type\":\"task\",\"labels\":[\"helix\",\"phase:build\",\"review-finding\",\"severity:medium\"],\"owner\":\"erik\",\"priority\":0,\"spec-id\":\".github/workflows/ci.yml\",\"status\":\"closed\",\"title\":\"integration: rebuild frontend dist before Playwright CI run\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-10T11:56:37.509906263Z\"}\n.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:870:{\"acceptance\":\"All review passes complete; findings filed as beads; AGENTS.md updated if needed\",\"archived_at\":\"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z\",\"claimed-at\":\"2026-04-10T06:20:16Z\",\"claimed-machine\":\"eitri\",\"claimed-pid\":\"1254206\",\"closing_commit_sha\":\"91f17a05b054b92d6d2b883bb4563e18fc2ce4d8\",\"created_at\":\"2026-04-10T06:20:09.572352968Z\",\"description\":\"\\u003ccontext-digest\\u003e\\n\\u003cprinciples\\u003eDesign for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions\\u003c/principles\\u003e\\n\\u003cconcerns\\u003ego-std | security-owasp | testing\\u003c/concerns\\u003e\\n\\u003cpractices\\u003eReview target is Go CLI tracker-provenance code plus acceptance tests. This trail is\nload-bearing data, not noise:\n\n- `chore: update tracker (execute-bead \u003cTIMESTAMP\u003e)` — one commit per\n  attempt; the timestamp is the attempt ID.\n- `Merge bead \u003cbead-id\u003e attempt \u003cTIMESTAMP\u003e- into \u003cbranch\u003e` — the merge\n  commit that lands a successful attempt from its isolated worktree.\n- `feat|fix|refactor|...: ... [ddx-\u003cid\u003e]` — the substantive bead work,\n  tagged with the bead ID.\n\nBead records store `closing_commit_sha` as a pointer back into git\nhistory; cost, latency, retry, and tier-escalation reports read these\ncommits directly. Any SHA rewrite breaks the pointers and destroys the\n`output = bead(input)` accuracy the system is built on.\n\n**NO HISTORY REWRITING on execute-bead branches.** The only acceptable\nmerge strategies are:\n\n1. **Plain fast-forward** — when the target is a strict ancestor of the\n   feature branch: `git merge --ff-only` + `git push`. No new commits.\n2. **Merge commit** — when divergence exists: `git merge --no-ff`.\n   Creates a 2-parent merge; the feature branch commits remain intact.\n\nNever use on an execute-bead branch:\n\n- `gh pr merge --squash` — collapses every attempt into one commit.\n- `gh pr merge --rebase` — GitHub's rebase-merge replays commits as NEW\n  SHAs, breaking `closing_commit_sha` pointers.\n- `git rebase -i` with `fixup`, `squash`, `drop`, or `reword` — rewrites\n  SHAs.\n- `git filter-branch` / `git filter-repo` stripping \"chore\" commits — same.\n- `git commit --amend` on any commit already in the trail — same.\n\nWhen in doubt, check `git log \u003cbranch\u003e --oneline | grep -E 'execute-bead|\\[ddx-'`.\nIf any match, preserve history on the merge.\n\n## Prohibited Actions\n\n- Do not edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` manually.\n- Do not add, remove, or rewrite bead rows with `apply_patch`, editors, scripts, or ad hoc JSONL manipulation.\n- Do not invent bead IDs or prefixes such as `hx-*` or `ddx-*`.\n- Do not treat nearby tracker entries as a naming pattern to copy.\n- Do not squash, rebase, or filter branches containing execute-bead commits (see Merge Policy above).\n\n## If The CLI Seems Insufficient\n\n- Prefer the nearest supported `ddx bead` command.\n- If the required tracker operation is not supported by `ddx bead`, stop and ask rather than editing tracker storage directly.\n\n## Verification\n\n- Use `ddx bead show \u003cid\u003e` to inspect one bead.\n- Use `ddx bead list`, `ddx bead ready`, and `ddx bead status` to inspect queue state.\n- Use `ddx bead --help` and `ddx bead create --help` before assuming a flag exists.\n\n## Skill Policy\n\n- Treat `SKILL.md` frontmatter as a strict interface, not freeform metadata.\n- Published DDx skills must use top-level YAML frontmatter fields `name` and `description`.\n- Use `argument-hint` only when the skill accepts a trailing positional or shorthand invocation hint.\n- Do not use nested `skill:` frontmatter for DDx repo skills.\n- Run `ddx skills check [path ...]` for reusable validation across repo skills and plugin skills.\n- Run `make skill-schema` after editing any file under `skills/*/SKILL.md` or `cli/internal/skills/*/SKILL.md`.\n\n\n## /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-d6e7772c-20260416T221424-87150f6f/CLAUDE.md\n\n# CLAUDE.md\n\nThis file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.\n\n## Project Overview\n\nDDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is the shared infrastructure platform for document-driven development. It provides the tools that developers and workflow systems (like HELIX) use to manage the documents AI agents consume to build software.\n\nDDx is one layer in a three-project stack:\n- **DDx** (this repo) — platform services: document library, bead tracker, agent service, personas, templates, git sync\n- **HELIX** (`~/Projects/helix`) — workflow methodology: phases, gates, supervisory dispatch, bounded actions\n- **Dun** (`~/Projects/dun`) — quality check runner: check discovery, execution, agent-friendly output\n\n## Architecture\n\nThis monorepo produces three artifacts:\n- **`ddx` CLI** (`/cli/`): Go binary — document library mgmt, bead tracker, agent dispatch, personas, templates, git sync\n- **`ddx-server`** (planned): Web server + MCP endpoints for document and bead access\n- **`ddx.github.io`** (`/website/`): Hugo promotional site\n\n### Key Components\n\n- `cli/` - Go CLI application source code\n  - `cmd/` - Cobra command implementations\n  - `internal/` - Internal packages (config, bead, persona, git, mcp, metaprompt, etc.)\n  - `main.go` - Application entry point\n- `website/` - Hugo site with Hextra theme\n- `library/` - DDx default plugin source (installed to `.ddx/plugins/ddx/` by `ddx init`)\n  - `templates/` - Project templates\n  - `patterns/` - Reusable code patterns\n  - `prompts/` - AI prompts and instructions\n  - `personas/` - AI persona definitions\n  - `mcp-servers/` - MCP server registry\n- `docs/helix/` - HELIX frame artifacts (vision, PRD, feature specs)\n- `scripts/` - Build and automation scripts\n\n## Development Commands\n\n### CLI Development (run from `/cli/` directory)\n\n```bash\n# Build and test\nmake build          # Build for current platform\nmake test           # Run Go tests\nmake lint           # Run golangci-lint (or go vet if not available)\nmake fmt            # Format Go code\n\n# Development workflow\nmake all            # Clean, deps, test, build\nmake dev            # Development mode with file watching (requires air)\nmake run ARGS=\"...\"  # Run CLI with arguments\nmake install        # Install locally to ~/.local/bin/ddx\n\n# Dependencies\nmake deps           # Install and tidy Go modules\nmake update-deps    # Update all dependencies\n\n# Multi-platform builds\nmake build-all      # Build for all platforms\nmake release        # Create release archives\n```\n\n### Frontend Development (SvelteKit)\n\nThe web UI is a SvelteKit application built with Bun. Run from the repository root:\n\n```bash\n# Install frontend dependencies and start dev server\ncd cli/internal/server/frontend \u0026\u0026 bun install \u0026\u0026 bun run dev\n\n# Generate GraphQL types from schema\nbun run houdini:generate\n\n# Run unit tests\nbun run test\n\n# Run e2e tests with Playwright\nbun run test:e2e\n```\n\nFrontend build output is embedded into the Go binary via `//go:embed`.\n\n### Project Structure Navigation\n\nThe CLI uses git subtree for managing the relationship between individual projects and the master DDx repository. The `.ddx.yml` configuration file defines:\n- Repository URL and branch\n- Included resources (prompts, scripts, templates, patterns)\n- Template variables and overrides\n- Git subtree settings\n\n### Key Patterns\n\n1. **Command Structure**: Each CLI command is implemented as a separate file in `cli/cmd/`\n2. **Configuration Management**: Uses Viper for config file handling with YAML format\n3. **Template Processing**: Variable substitution system for customizing templates\n4. **Git Integration**: Built on git subtree for reliable version control and contribution workflows\n5. **Cross-Platform Support**: Makefile supports building for multiple platforms (macOS, Linux, Windows)\n\n## Architectural Principles\n\n**CRITICAL**: The DDx CLI follows the principle of \"Extensibility Through Composition\" - keep the CLI core minimal and add features through library resources.\n\n1. **Platform Services in CLI, Opinions in Workflows**:\n   - CLI owns: document library, bead tracker, agent dispatch, personas, templates, git sync\n   - Workflow tools own: phase enforcement, supervisory loops, methodology-specific validation\n   - DDx provides primitives; HELIX and others provide opinions\n\n2. **Feature Addition Pattern**:\n   - Document-type resources go in the library (personas, patterns, templates)\n   - Platform capabilities go in the CLI (beads, agent service, MCP)\n   - Workflow-specific logic stays in workflow tools (HELIX, etc.)\n\n## Testing and Quality\n\n**CRITICAL**: All tests are release-critical and must pass before committing:\n\n```bash\n# Run full test suite - all tests must pass\ncd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -v ./cmd\n```\n\nEvery test validates critical functionality and must pass before any release.\n\n- Go tests are in `*_test.go` files alongside source code\n- Linting uses golangci-lint (fallback to go vet)\n- Code formatting with `go fmt`\n- Cross-platform compatibility is maintained\n\n### Pre-commit Checks\n\nThe project uses Lefthook for git hooks. To run pre-commit checks manually:\n\n```bash\n# Run all pre-commit checks\nlefthook run pre-commit\n\n# Or stage files and run checks\ngit add \u003cfiles\u003e\nlefthook run pre-commit\n```\n\nPre-commit checks include:\n- Secrets detection\n- Binary file prevention\n- Debug statement detection\n- Merge conflict detection\n- DDx configuration validation\n- Go linting, formatting, building, and testing\n\n## CLI Command Overview\n\nThe CLI follows a noun-verb command structure for clarity and consistency:\n\n**Core Commands:**\n- `ddx init` - Initialize DDx in a project\n- `ddx doctor` - Check installation health and diagnose issues\n- `ddx upgrade` - Upgrade DDx binary to latest release version\n- `ddx update` - Update toolkit resources from master repository\n- `ddx contribute` - Share improvements back to community\n- `ddx status` - Show version and sync status\n\n**Bead Tracker:**\n- `ddx bead create/show/update/close` - Work item CRUD\n- `ddx bead list/ready/blocked/status` - Query and filter beads\n- `ddx bead dep add/remove/tree` - Dependency DAG management\n- `ddx bead import/export` - JSONL interchange with bd/br\n\n**Agent Service:**\n- `ddx agent run --harness=\u003cname\u003e --prompt \u003cfile\u003e` - Invoke an AI agent\n- `ddx agent run --quorum=majority --harnesses=a,b` - Multi-agent consensus\n- `ddx agent execute-bead \u003cid\u003e [--from \u003crev\u003e] [--no-merge]` - Run agent in isolated worktree, merge or preserve result\n- `ddx agent list` - Show available harnesses\n- `ddx agent doctor` - Harness health check\n- `ddx agent log` - Session history\n\n**Resource Commands:**\n- `ddx prompts list/show` - AI prompts\n- `ddx templates list/apply` - Project templates\n- `ddx persona list/show/bind` - AI personas\n- `ddx mcp list/install` - MCP servers\n\n**Embedded Utilities:**\n- `ddx jq \u003cfilter\u003e [file...]` - Embedded jq processor (via gojq), no external jq required\n\n## Persona System\n\nDDX includes a persona system that provides consistent AI personalities for different roles:\n\n- **Personas**: Reusable AI personality templates (e.g., `strict-code-reviewer`, `test-engineer-tdd`)\n- **Roles**: Abstract functions that personas fulfill (e.g., `code-reviewer`, `test-engineer`)\n- **Bindings**: Project-specific mappings between roles and personas in `.ddx.yml`\n\nPersonas enable consistent, high-quality AI interactions across team members and projects. Projects bind specific personas to roles. See `library/personas/` for available personas and `library/personas/README.md` for detailed documentation.\n\n\u003c!-- DDX-META-PROMPT:START --\u003e\n\u003c!-- Source: claude/system-prompts/focused.md --\u003e\n# System Instructions\n\n**Execute ONLY what is requested:**\n\n- **YAGNI** (You Aren't Gonna Need It): Implement only specified features. Populated\n\t// by the orchestrator from known patterns; see ClassifyFailureMode and\n\t// the FailureMode* constants in execute_bead_status.go.\n\tFailureMode string `json:\"failure_mode,omitempty\"`\n\n\tExecutionDir string `json:\"execution_dir,omitempty\"`\n\tPromptFile   string `json:\"prompt_file,omitempty\"`\n\tManifestFile string `json:\"manifest_file,omitempty\"`\n\tResultFile   string `json:\"result_file,omitempty\"`\n\tUsageFile    string `json:\"usage_file,omitempty\"`\n\n\tStartedAt  time.Time `json:\"started_at\"`\n\tFinishedAt time.Time `json:\"finished_at\"`\n}\n\n// BeadEventAppender records append-only evidence events on a bead.\n// Implemented by *bead.Store — kept as a minimal interface so the agent\n// package does not need to import a concrete store type in tests.\ntype BeadEventAppender interface {\n\tAppendEvent(id string, event bead.BeadEvent) error\n}\n\n// ExecuteBeadOptions holds all parameters for an execute-bead worker run.\ntype ExecuteBeadOptions struct {\n\tFromRev    string // base git revision (default: HEAD)\n\tHarness    string\n\tModel      string\n\tProvider   string // explicit provider name; passed through to agent as -provider\n\tModelRef   string // catalog model-ref; passed through to agent as -model-ref\n\tEffort     string\n\tPromptFile string // override prompt file (auto-generated if empty)\n\tWorkerID   string // from DDX_WORKER_ID env or caller\n\t// BeadEvents, when non-nil, receives a kind:routing evidence entry after\n\t// the agent run completes. This is the hook that feeds the cost-tiered\n\t// routing analytics described in FEAT-routing-visibility.\n\tBeadEvents BeadEventAppender\n}\n\n// GitOps abstracts the git operations required by the worker.\n// Merge and UpdateRef are intentionally excluded — those belong to the\n// parent-side orchestrator (OrchestratorGitOps).\ntype GitOps interface {\n\tHeadRev(dir string) (string, error)\n\tResolveRev(dir, rev string) (string, error)\n\tWorktreeAdd(dir, wtPath, rev string) error\n\tWorktreeRemove(dir, wtPath string) error\n\tWorktreeList(dir string) ([]string, error)\n\tWorktreePrune(dir string) error\n\tIsDirty(dir string) (bool, error)\n\t// SynthesizeCommit stages real file changes (excluding harness noise paths) and\n\t// commits them using msg as the commit message. Returns (true, nil) when a\n\t// commit was made, (false, nil) when there was nothing real to commit (all\n\t// dirty files were noise), and (false, err) on failure.\n\tSynthesizeCommit(dir, msg string) (bool, error)\n}\n\n// AgentRunner runs an agent with the given options.\ntype AgentRunner interface {\n\tRun(opts RunOptions) (*Result, error)\n}\n\n// Artifact paths for an execute-bead attempt.\ntype executeBeadArtifacts struct {\n\tDirAbs      string\n\tDirRel      string\n\tPromptAbs   string\n\tPromptRel   string\n\tManifestAbs string\n\tManifestRel string\n\tResultAbs   string\n\tResultRel   string\n\tChecksAbs   string\n\tChecksRel   string\n\tUsageAbs    string\n\tUsageRel    string\n}\n\ntype executeBeadManifest struct {\n\tAttemptID string                    `json:\"attempt_id\"`\n\tWorkerID  string                    `json:\"worker_id,omitempty\"`\n\tBeadID    string                    `json:\"bead_id\"`\n\tBaseRev   string                    `json:\"base_rev\"`\n\tCreatedAt time.Time                 `json:\"created_at\"`\n\tRequested executeBeadRequested      `json:\"requested\"`\n\tBead      executeBeadManifestBead   `json:\"bead\"`\n\tGoverning []executeBeadGoverningRef `json:\"governing,omitempty\"`\n\tPaths     executeBeadArtifactPaths  `json:\"paths\"`\n}\n\ntype executeBeadRequested struct {\n\tHarness  string `json:\"harness,omitempty\"`\n\tModel    string `json:\"model,omitempty\"`\n\tProvider string `json:\"provider,omitempty\"`\n\tModelRef string `json:\"model_ref,omitempty\"`\n\tEffort   string `json:\"effort,omitempty\"`\n\tPrompt   string `json:\"prompt,omitempty\"`\n}\n\ntype executeBeadManifestBead struct {\n\tID          string         `json:\"id\"`\n\tTitle       string         `json:\"title\"`\n\tDescription string         `json:\"description,omitempty\"`\n\tAcceptance  string         `json:\"acceptance,omitempty\"`\n\tParent      string         `json:\"parent,omitempty\"`\n\tLabels      []string       `json:\"labels,omitempty\"`\n\tMetadata    map[string]any `json:\"metadata,omitempty\"`\n}\n\ntype executeBeadGoverningRef struct {\n\tID    string `json:\"id\"`\n\tPath  string `json:\"path\"`\n\tTitle string `json:\"title,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// GoverningRef is the exported alias for executeBeadGoverningRef, for use\n// outside the agent package (e.g. cmd/bead_review.go).\ntype GoverningRef = executeBeadGoverningRef\n\ntype executeBeadArtifactPaths struct {\n\tDir      string `json:\"dir\"`\n\tPrompt   string `json:\"prompt\"`\n\tManifest string `json:\"manifest\"`\n\tResult   string `json:\"result\"`\n\tChecks   string `json:\"checks,omitempty\"`\n\tUsage    string `json:\"usage,omitempty\"`\n\tWorktree string `json:\"worktree\"`\n}\n\n// Constants for worktree and artifact paths.\nconst (\n\tExecuteBeadWtDir       = \".ddx\" // legacy; kept for compatibility\n\tExecuteBeadWtPrefix    = \".execute-bead-wt-\"\n\tExecuteBeadArtifactDir = \".ddx/executions\"\n\n\t// ExecuteBeadTmpSubdir is the subdirectory under $TMPDIR in which\n\t// execute-bead creates its isolated worktrees. Keeping them outside\n\t// the project tree prevents child processes (tests, hooks) running\n\t// inside the worktree from mutating the parent repository's\n\t// .git/config via inherited GIT_DIR.\n\tExecuteBeadTmpSubdir = \"ddx-exec-wt\"\n)\n\n// executeBeadWorktreePath returns the absolute path where an execute-bead\n// isolated worktree for (beadID, attemptID) should live.\nfunc executeBeadWorktreePath(beadID, attemptID string) string {\n\tbase := os.Getenv(\"DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR\")\n\tif base == \"\" {\n\t\tbase = filepath.Join(os.TempDir(), ExecuteBeadTmpSubdir)\n\t}\n\treturn filepath.Join(base, ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+beadID+\"-\"+attemptID)\n}\n\n// RealGitOps implements GitOps via os/exec git commands.\ntype RealGitOps struct{}\n\nfunc (r *RealGitOps) HeadRev(dir string) (string, error) {\n\treturn r.ResolveRev(dir, \"HEAD\")\n}\n\nfunc (r *RealGitOps) ResolveRev(dir, rev string) (string, error) {\n\tout, err := osexec.Command(\"git\", \"-C\", dir, \"rev-parse\", rev).Output()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"git rev-parse %s: %w\", rev, err)\n\t}\n\treturn strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil\n}\n\nfunc (r *RealGitOps) WorktreeAdd(dir, wtPath, rev string) error {\n\tout, err := osexec.Command(\"git\", \"-C\", dir, \"worktree\", \"add\", \"--detach\", wtPath, rev).CombinedOutput()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"git worktree add: %s: %w\", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (r *RealGitOps) WorktreeRemove(dir, wtPath string) error {\n\t_ = osexec.Command(\"git\", \"-C\", dir, \"worktree\", \"--porcelain\").Output()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"git worktree list: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tvar paths []string\n\tfor _, line := range strings.Split(string(out), \"\\n\") {\n\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(line, \"worktree \") {\n\t\t\tpaths = append(paths, strings.TrimPrefix(line, \"worktree \"))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn paths, nil\n}\n\nfunc (r *RealGitOps) WorktreePrune(dir
