files).\nfunc (r *RealGitOps) IsDirty(dir string) (bool, error) {\n\tout, _ := osexec.Command(\"git\", \"-C\", dir, \"status\", \"--porcelain\").Output()\n\treturn len(bytes.TrimSpace(out)) \u003e 0, nil\n}\n\n// SynthesizeCommit stages real file changes, explicitly excluding harness noise\n// paths, and creates a commit with msg as the commit message. Returns (true, nil)\n// when a commit was made, (false, nil) when nothing real remained to commit\n// after exclusions, and (false, err) on failure.\nfunc (r *RealGitOps) SynthesizeCommit(dir, msg string) (bool, error) {\n\taddArgs := []string{\n\t\t\"-C\", dir, \"add\", \"-A\", \"--\",\n\t\t\".\",\n\t\t\":(exclude).ddx/agent-logs\",\n\t\t\":(exclude).ddx/workers\",\n\t\t\":(exclude).ddx/executions/*/embedded\",\n\t\t\":(exclude).ddx/executions/*/no_changes_rationale.txt\",\n\t\t\":(exclude).claude/skills\",\n\t\t\":(exclude).agents/skills\",\n\t}\n\tif err := osexec.Command(\"git\", addArgs...).Run(); err != nil {\n\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"staging changes: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tstatusOut, _ := osexec.Command(\"git\", \"-C\", dir, \"diff\", \"--cached\", \"--name-only\").Output()\n\tif len(bytes.TrimSpace(statusOut)) == 0 {\n\t\treturn false, nil\n\t}\n\tif msg == \"\" {\n\t\tmsg = \"chore: execute-bead synthesized result commit\"\n\t}\n\tout, err := osexec.Command(\"git\", \"-C\", dir, \"commit\", \"-m\", msg).CombinedOutput()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn false, fmt.Errorf(\"synthesize commit: %s: %w\", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)\n\t}\n\treturn true, nil\n}\n\n// GenerateAttemptID generates a unique attempt identifier.\nfunc GenerateAttemptID() string {\n\tb := make([]byte, 4)\n\t_, _ = rand.Read(b)\n\treturn time.Now().UTC().Format(\"20060102T150405\") + \"-\" + hex.EncodeToString(b)\n}\n\n// GenerateSessionID generates a short session ID for the agent log.\nfunc GenerateSessionID() string {\n\tb := make([]byte, 4)\n\t_, _ = rand.Read(b)\n\treturn \"eb-\" + hex.EncodeToString(b)\n}\n\n// appendBeadRoutingEvidence records a kind:routing evidence entry on the bead\n// after an agent run. It is a best-effort operation — errors are silently\n// ignored so a store failure never aborts the main execute-bead flow.\nfunc appendBeadRoutingEvidence(appender BeadEventAppender, beadID, harness, provider, model, routeReason, baseURL string) {\n\tif appender == nil || beadID == \"\" {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tresolvedProvider := provider\n\tif resolvedProvider == \"\" {\n\t\tresolvedProvider = harness\n\t}\n\ttype routingBody struct {\n\t\tResolvedProvider string   `json:\"resolved_provider\"`\n\t\tResolvedModel    string   `json:\"resolved_model,omitempty\"`\n\t\tRouteReason      string   `json:\"route_reason,omitempty\"`\n\t\tFallbackChain    []string `json:\"fallback_chain\"`\n\t\tBaseURL          string   `json:\"base_url,omitempty\"`\n\t}\n\tbody := routingBody{\n\t\tResolvedProvider: resolvedProvider,\n\t\tResolvedModel:    model,\n\t\tRouteReason:      routeReason,\n\t\tFallbackChain:    []string{},\n\t\tBaseURL:          baseURL,\n\t}\n\tdata, err := json.Marshal(body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tsummary := fmt.Sprintf(\"provider=%s\", resolvedProvider)\n\tif model != \"\" {\n\t\tsummary += fmt.Sprintf(\" model=%s\", model)\n\t}\n\tif routeReason != \"\" {\n\t\tsummary += fmt.Sprintf(\" reason=%s\", routeReason)\n\t}\n\t_ = appender.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{\n\t\tKind:    \"routing\",\n\t\tSummary: summary,\n\t\tBody:    string(data),\n\t\tActor:   \"ddx\",\n\t\tSource:  \"ddx agent execute-bead\",\n\t})\n}\n\n// ExecuteBead is the thin worker: it creates an isolated worktree, constructs\n// the agent prompt from bead context, runs the agent harness, synthesizes a\n// commit if the agent left uncommitted changes, then cleans up the worktree\n// and returns the result. It classifies outcomes as exactly one of:\n//\n//   - task_succeeded: agent exited 0 and produced one or more commits\n//   - task_failed:    agent exited non-zero\n//   - task_no_changes: agent exited 0 but made no commits\n//\n// Merge, UpdateRef, gate evaluation, preserve-ref management, and orphan\n// recovery are the parent's responsibility (see LandBeadResult, RecoverOrphans).\nfunc ExecuteBead(projectRoot string, beadID string, opts ExecuteBeadOptions, gitOps GitOps, runner AgentRunner) (*ExecuteBeadResult, error) {\n\tattemptID := GenerateAttemptID()\n\tif opts.WorkerID == \"\" {\n\t\topts.WorkerID = os.Getenv(\"DDX_WORKER_ID\")\n\t}\n\n\twtPath := executeBeadWorktreePath(beadID, attemptID)\n\tif mkErr := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(wtPath), 0o755); mkErr != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"creating execute-bead worktree parent dir: %w\", mkErr)\n\t}\n\n\t// Commit beads.jsonl before spawning worktree so the worktree snapshot\n\t// includes any bead metadata updates (e.g. spec-id).\n\tif err := CommitTracker(projectRoot); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\t// Resolve base revision after the tracker commit.\n\tbaseRev, err := resolveBase(gitOps, projectRoot, opts.FromRev)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\t// Create the isolated worktree. Orphan recovery is the parent's responsibility\n\t// (call RecoverOrphans before invoking workers).\n\tif err := gitOps.WorktreeAdd(projectRoot, wtPath, baseRev); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"creating isolated worktree: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tdefer func() {\n\t\t_ = gitOps.WorktreeRemove(projectRoot, wtPath)\n\t}()\n\n\t// Repair project-local skill symlinks whose targets do not resolve inside\n\t// the freshly created worktree.\n\t_ = materializeWorktreeSkills(wtPath)\n\n\t// Prepare artifacts (context load, prompt generation).\n\tartifacts, err := prepareArtifacts(projectRoot, wtPath, beadID, attemptID, baseRev, opts)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tres := \u0026ExecuteBeadResult{\n\t\t\tBeadID:    beadID,\n\t\t\tAttemptID: attemptID,\n\t\t\tWorkerID:  opts.WorkerID,\n\t\t\tBaseRev:   baseRev,\n\t\t\tResultRev: baseRev, // no commits; ResultRev == BaseRev signals no output\n\t\t\tExitCode:  1,\n\t\t\tError:     err.Error(),\n\t\t\tOutcome:   ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed,\n\t\t}\n\t\tif abInfo, _ := os.Stat(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, attemptID)); abInfo != nil \u0026\u0026 abInfo.IsDir() {\n\t\t\tres.ExecutionDir = filepath.Join(ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, attemptID)\n\t\t}\n\t\tres.FailureMode = ClassifyFailureMode(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Error)\n\t\tpopulateWorkerStatus(res)\n\t\t_ = writeArtifactJSON(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ExecuteBeadArtifactDir, attemptID, \"result.json\"), res)\n\t\treturn res, fmt.Errorf(\"execute-bead context load: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Pre-create the execution bundle dir in the worktree so the agent can write\n\t// artifacts (e.g. no_changes_rationale.txt) without needing to create the\n\t// directory itself. Failures are non-fatal: the agent can create it on demand.\n\t_ = os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(wtPath, artifacts.DirRel), 0o755)\n\n\t// Redirect per-run session/telemetry output into the DDx-owned execution\n\t// bundle so the embedded harness does not accumulate state at the worktree root.\n\tembeddedStateDir := filepath.Join(artifacts.DirAbs, \"embedded\")\n\tif err := os.MkdirAll(embeddedStateDir, 0o755); err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"creating embedded state dir: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tsessionID := GenerateSessionID()\n\tstartedAt := time.Now().UTC()\n\n\tagentResult, agentErr := runner.Run(RunOptions{\n\t\tHarness:       opts.Harness,\n\t\tPrompt:        \"\",\n\t\tPromptFile:    artifacts.PromptAbs,\n\t\tModel:         opts.Model,\n\t\tProvider:      opts.Provider,\n\t\tModelRef:      opts.ModelRef,\n\t\tEffort:        opts.Effort,\n\t\tWorkDir:       wtPath,\n\t\tPermissions:   \"unrestricted\", // isolated worktree; writes must not require approval\n\t\tSessionLogDir: embeddedStateDir,\n\t\tCorrelation: map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\"bead_id\":    beadID,\n\t\t\t\"base_rev\":   baseRev,\n\t\t\t\"attempt_id\": attemptID,\n\t\t\t\"session_id\": sessionID,\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\tfinishedAt := time.Now().UTC()\n\n\texitCode := 0\n\ttokens := 0\n\tinputTokens := 0\n\toutputTokens := 0\n\tcostUSD := 0.0\n\tresultModel := opts.Model\n\tresultHarness := opts.Harness\n\tresultProvider := \"\"\n\tagentErrMsg := \"\"\n\tif agentResult != nil {\n\t\texitCode = agentResult.ExitCode\n\t\ttokens = agentResult.Tokens\n\t\tinputTokens = agentResult.InputTokens\n\t\toutputTokens = agentResult.OutputTokens\n\t\tcostUSD = agentResult.CostUSD\n\t\tif agentResult.Error != \"\" {\n\t\t\tagentErrMsg = agentResult.Error\n\t\t}\n\t\tif agentResult.Model != \"\" {\n\t\t\tresultModel = agentResult.Model\n\t\t}\n\t\tif agentResult.Provider != \"\" {\n\t\t\tresultProvider = agentResult.Provider\n\t\t}\n\t\tif agentResult.Harness != \"\" {\n\t\t\tresultHarness = agentResult.Harness\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif agentErr != nil {\n\t\tif exitCode == 0 {\n\t\t\texitCode = 1\n\t\t}\n\t\tagentErrMsg = agentErr.Error()\n\t}\n\n\t// Capture routing evidence from the agent result. These fields are\n\t// populated by RunAgent (embedded harness) and RunScript (script harness).\n\trouteReason := \"\"\n\trouteBaseURL := \"\"\n\tif agentResult != nil {\n\t\trouteReason = agentResult.RouteReason\n\t\trouteBaseURL = agentResult.ResolvedBaseURL\n\t}\n\n\t// Get the HEAD of the worktree after the agent ran.\n\tresultRev, revErr := gitOps.HeadRev(wtPath)\n\tif revErr != nil {\n\t\tres := \u0026ExecuteBeadResult{\n\t\t\tBeadID:       beadID,\n\t\t\tAttemptID:    attemptID,\n\t\t\tWorkerID:     opts.WorkerID,\n\t\t\tBaseRev:      baseRev,\n\t\t\tResultRev:    baseRev, // no commits readable; treat as no output\n\t\t\tHarness:      resultHarness,\n\t\t\tProvider:     resultProvider,\n\t\t\tModel:        resultModel,\n\t\t\tSessionID:    sessionID,\n\t\t\tDurationMS:   int(finishedAt.Sub(startedAt).Milliseconds()),\n\t\t\tTokens:       tokens,\n\t\t\tCostUSD:      costUSD,\n\t\t\tExitCode:     1,\n\t\t\tError:        agentErrMsg,\n\t\t\tReason:       revErr.Error(), // HeadRev failure; orchestrator prefers this over Error for Reason\n\t\t\tExecutionDir: artifacts.DirRel,\n\t\t\tPromptFile:   artifacts.PromptRel,\n\t\t\tManifestFile: artifacts.ManifestRel,\n\t\t\tResultFile:   artifacts.ResultRel,\n\t\t\tStartedAt:    startedAt,\n\t\t\tFinishedAt:   finishedAt,\n\t\t\tOutcome:      ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed,\n\t\t}\n\t\tres.FailureMode = ClassifyFailureMode(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Error)\n\t\tpopulateWorkerStatus(res)\n\t\t_ = writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.ResultAbs, res)\n\t\treturn res, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read worktree HEAD: %w\", revErr)\n\t}\n\n\t// Write usage.json when the harness reports token usage or cost.\n\t// Done before SynthesizeCommit so usage data is available in the\n\t// preliminary result written for commit-message sourcing.\n\tvar usageFileRel string\n\tif tokens \u003e 0 || costUSD \u003e 0 {\n\t\tusage := executeBeadUsage{\n\t\t\tAttemptID:    attemptID,\n\t\t\tHarness:      resultHarness,\n\t\t\tProvider:     resultProvider,\n\t\t\tModel:        resultModel,\n\t\t\tTokens:       tokens,\n\t\t\tInputTokens:  inputTokens,\n\t\t\tOutputTokens: outputTokens,\n\t\t\tCostUSD:      costUSD,\n\t\t}\n\t\tif writeErr := writeArtifactJSON(artifacts.UsageAbs, usage); writeErr == nil {\n\t\t\tusageFileRel = artifacts.UsageRel\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Synthesize a commit when the agent left tracked edits without committing.\n\t// Only do so for real changes — harness noise paths are excluded. This trail is\nload-bearing data, not noise:\n\n- `chore: update tracker (execute-bead \u003cTIMESTAMP\u003e)` — one commit per\n  attempt; the timestamp is the attempt ID.\n- `Merge bead \u003cbead-id\u003e attempt \u003cTIMESTAMP\u003e- into \u003cbranch\u003e` — the merge\n  commit that lands a successful attempt from its isolated worktree.\n- `feat|fix|refactor|...: ... [ddx-\u003cid\u003e]` — the substantive bead work,\n  tagged with the bead ID.\n\nBead records store `closing_commit_sha` as a pointer back into git\nhistory; cost, latency, retry, and tier-escalation reports read these\ncommits directly. Any SHA rewrite breaks the pointers and destroys the\n`output = bead(input)` accuracy the system is built on.\n\n**NO HISTORY REWRITING on execute-bead branches.** The only acceptable\nmerge strategies are:\n\n1. **Plain fast-forward** — when the target is a strict ancestor of the\n   feature branch: `git merge --ff-only` + `git push`. No new commits.\n2. **Merge commit** — when divergence exists: `git merge --no-ff`.\n   Creates a 2-parent merge; the feature branch commits remain intact.\n\nNever use on an execute-bead branch:\n\n- `gh pr merge --squash` — collapses every attempt into one commit.\n- `gh pr merge --rebase` — GitHub's rebase-merge replays commits as NEW\n  SHAs, breaking `closing_commit_sha` pointers.\n- `git rebase -i` with `fixup`, `squash`, `drop`, or `reword` — rewrites\n  SHAs.\n- `git filter-branch` / `git filter-repo` stripping \"chore\" commits — same.\n- `git commit --amend` on any commit already in the trail — same.\n\nWhen in doubt, check `git log \u003cbranch\u003e --oneline | grep -E 'execute-bead|\\[ddx-'`.\nIf any match, preserve history on the merge.\n\n## Prohibited Actions\n\n- Do not edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` manually.\n- Do not add, remove, or rewrite bead rows with `apply_patch`, editors, scripts, or ad hoc JSONL manipulation.\n- Do not invent bead IDs or prefixes such as `hx-*` or `ddx-*`.\n- Do not treat nearby tracker entries as a naming pattern to copy.\n- Do not squash, rebase, or filter branches containing execute-bead commits (see Merge Policy above).\n\n## If The CLI --help` and `ddx bead create --help` before assuming a flag exists.\n\n## Skill Policy\n\n- Treat `SKILL.md` frontmatter as a strict interface, not freeform metadata.\n- Published DDx skills must use Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.\n\n## Project Overview\n\nDDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is the shared infrastructure platform for document-driven development. It provides the tools that developers and workflow systems (like HELIX) use to manage the documents AI agents consume to build software.\n\nDDx is one layer in a three-project stack:\n- **DDx** (this repo) — platform services: document library, bead tracker, agent service, personas, templates, git sync\n- **HELIX** (`~/Projects/helix`) — workflow methodology: phases, gates, supervisory dispatch, bounded actions\n- **Dun** (`~/Projects/dun`) — quality check runner: check discovery, execution, agent-friendly output\n\n## Architecture\n\nThis monorepo produces three artifacts:\n- **`ddx` CLI** (`/cli/`): Go binary — document library mgmt, bead tracker, agent dispatch, personas, templates, git sync\n- **`ddx-server`** (planned): Web server + MCP endpoints for document and bead access\n- **`ddx.github.io`** (`/website/`): Hugo promotional site\n\n### Key Components\n\n- `cli/` - Go CLI application source code\n  - `cmd/` - Cobra command implementations\n  - `internal/` - Internal packages (config, bead, persona, git, mcp, metaprompt, etc.)\n  - `main.go` - Application entry point\n- `website/` - Hugo site with Hextra theme\n- `library/` - DDx default plugin source (installed to `.ddx/plugins/ddx/` by `ddx init`)\n  - `templates/` - Project templates\n  - `patterns/` - Reusable code patterns\n  - `prompts/` - AI prompts and instructions\n  - `personas/` - AI persona definitions\n  - `mcp-servers/` - MCP server registry\n- `docs/helix/` - HELIX frame artifacts (vision, PRD, feature specs)\n- `scripts/` - Build and automation scripts\n\n## Development Commands\n\n### CLI Development (run from `/cli/` directory)\n\n```bash\n# Build and test\nmake build          # Build for current platform\nmake test           # Run Go tests\nmake lint           # Run golangci-lint (or go vet if not available)\nmake fmt            # Format Go code\n\n# Development workflow\nmake all            # Clean, deps, test, build\nmake dev            # Development mode with file watching (requires air)\nmake run ARGS=\"...\"  # Run CLI with arguments\nmake install        # Install locally to ~/.local/bin/ddx\n\n# Dependencies\nmake deps           # Install and tidy Go modules\nmake update-deps    # Update all dependencies\n\n# Multi-platform builds\nmake build-all      # Build for all platforms\nmake release        # Create release archives\n```\n\n### Frontend Development (SvelteKit)\n\nThe web UI is a SvelteKit application built with Bun. Run from the repository root:\n\n```bash\n# Install frontend dependencies and start dev server\ncd cli/internal/server/frontend \u0026\u0026 bun install \u0026\u0026 bun run dev\n\n# Generate GraphQL types from schema\nbun run houdini:generate\n\n# Run unit tests\nbun run test\n\n# Run e2e tests with Playwright\nbun run test:e2e\n```\n\nFrontend build output is embedded into the Go binary via `//go:embed`.\n\n### Project Structure Navigation\n\nThe CLI uses git subtree for managing the relationship between individual projects and the master DDx repository. The `.ddx.yml` configuration file defines:\n- Repository URL and branch\n- Included resources (prompts, scripts, templates, patterns)\n- Template variables and overrides\n- Git subtree settings\n\n### Key Patterns\n\n1. **Command Structure**: Each CLI command is implemented as a separate file in `cli/cmd/`\n2. **Configuration Management**: Uses Viper for config file handling with YAML format\n3. **Template Processing**: Variable substitution system for customizing templates\n4. **Git Integration**: Built on git subtree for reliable version control and contribution workflows\n5. **Cross-Platform Support**: Makefile supports building for multiple platforms (macOS, Linux, Windows)\n\n## Architectural Principles\n\n**CRITICAL**: The DDx CLI follows the principle of \"Extensibility Through Composition\" - keep the CLI core minimal and add features through library resources.\n\n1. **Platform Services in CLI, Opinions in Workflows**:\n   - CLI owns: document library, bead tracker, agent dispatch, personas, templates, git sync\n   - Workflow tools own: phase enforcement, supervisory loops, methodology-specific validation\n   - DDx provides primitives; HELIX and others provide opinions\n\n2. **Feature Addition Pattern**:\n   - Document-type resources go in the library (personas, patterns, templates)\n   - Platform capabilities go in the CLI (beads, agent service, MCP)\n   - Workflow-specific logic stays in workflow tools (HELIX, etc.)\n\n## Testing and Quality\n\n**CRITICAL**: All tests are release-critical and must pass before committing:\n\n```bash\n# Run full test suite - all tests must pass\ncd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -v ./cmd\n```\n\nEvery test validates critical functionality and must pass before any release.\n\n- Go tests are in `*_test.go` files alongside source code\n- Linting uses golangci-lint (fallback to go vet)\n- Code formatting with `go fmt`\n- Cross-platform compatibility is maintained\n\n### Pre-commit Checks\n\nThe project uses Lefthook for git hooks. To run pre-commit checks manually:\n\n```bash\n# Run all pre-commit checks\nlefthook run pre-commit\n\n# Or stage files and run checks\ngit add \u003cfiles\u003e\nlefthook run pre-commit\n```\n\nPre-commit checks include:\n- Secrets detection\n- Binary file prevention\n- Debug statement detection\n- Merge conflict detection\n- DDx configuration validation\n- Go linting, formatting, building, and testing\n\n## CLI Command Overview\n\nThe CLI follows a noun-verb command structure for clarity and consistency:\n\n**Core Commands:**\n- `ddx init` - Initialize DDx in a project\n- `ddx doctor` - Check installation health and diagnose issues\n- `ddx upgrade` - Upgrade DDx binary to latest release version\n- `ddx update` - Update toolkit resources from master repository\n- `ddx contribute` - Share improvements back to community\n- `ddx status` - Show version and sync status\n\n**Bead Tracker:**\n- `ddx bead create/show/update/close` - Work item CRUD\n- `ddx bead list/ready/blocked/status` - Query and filter beads\n- `ddx bead dep add/remove/tree` - Dependency DAG management\n- `ddx bead import/export` - JSONL interchange with bd/br\n\n**Agent Service:**\n- `ddx agent run --harness=\u003cname\u003e --prompt \u003cfile\u003e` - Invoke an AI agent\n- `ddx agent run --quorum=majority --harnesses=a,b` - Multi-agent consensus\n- `ddx agent execute-bead \u003cid\u003e [--from \u003crev\u003e] [--no-merge]` - Run agent in isolated worktree, merge or preserve result\n- `ddx agent list` - Show available harnesses\n- `ddx agent doctor` - Harness health check\n- `ddx agent log` - Session history\n\n**Resource Commands:**\n- `ddx prompts list/show` - AI prompts\n- `ddx templates list/apply` - Project templates\n- `ddx persona list/show/bind` - AI personas\n- `ddx mcp list/install` - MCP servers\n\n**Embedded Utilities:**\n- `ddx jq \u003cfilter\u003e [file...]` - Embedded jq processor (via gojq), no external jq required\n\n## Persona System\n\nDDX includes a persona system that provides consistent AI personalities for different roles:\n\n- **Personas**: Reusable AI personality templates (e.g., `strict-code-reviewer`, `test-engineer-tdd`)\n- **Roles**: Abstract functions that personas fulfill (e.g., `code-reviewer`, `test-engineer`)\n- **Bindings**: Project-specific mappings between roles and personas in `.ddx.yml`\n\nPersonas enable consistent, high-quality AI interactions across team members and projects. Projects bind specific personas to roles. cmd\n}\n\n// beadAutoCommit commits .ddx/beads.jsonl if git.auto_commit is \"always\".\n// The operation string describes what happened (e.g. \"create ddx-abc123\").\n// Errors are silently ignored — auto-commit is best-effort.\n// When a commit lands, the resulting SHA is returned.\nfunc (f *CommandFactory) beadAutoCommit(operation string) string {\n\tworkspaceRoot := f.beadWorkspaceRoot()\n\tif workspaceRoot == \"\" {\n\t\tworkspaceRoot = f.WorkingDir\n\t}\n\n\tcfg, err := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(workspaceRoot)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\tif cfg.Git == nil {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\tacCfg := gitpkg.AutoCommitConfig{\n\t\tAutoCommit:   cfg.Git.AutoCommit,\n\t\tCommitPrefix: cfg.Git.CommitPrefix,\n\t}\n\tbeadsFile := filepath.Join(workspaceRoot, \".ddx\", \"beads.jsonl\")\n\tsha, _ := gitpkg.AutoCommit(beadsFile, \"beads\", operation, acCfg)\n\treturn sha\n}\n\nfunc (f *CommandFactory) resolveCommitSHA(commitSHA string) (string, error) {\n\tif commitSHA == \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"\", nil\n\t}\n\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)\n\tdefer cancel()\n\n\trepoDir := f.WorkingDir\n\tif repoDir == \"\" {\n\t\trepoDir = \".\"\n\t}\n\n\tif isFullCommitSHA(commitSHA) \u0026\u0026 !gitpkg.IsRepository(repoDir) {\n\t\treturn commitSHA, nil\n\t}\n\n\tcmd := gitpkg.Command(ctx, repoDir, \"rev-parse\", \"--verify\", commitSHA+\"^{commit}\")\n\tout, err := cmd.Output()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"git rev-parse %s: %w\", commitSHA, err)\n\t}\n\treturn strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil\n}\n\nfunc isFullCommitSHA(commitSHA string) bool {\n\tif len(commitSHA) != 40 {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\tfor i := 0; i \u003c len(commitSHA); i++ {\n\t\tc := commitSHA[i]\n\t\tif c \u003e= '0' \u0026\u0026 c \u003c= '9' {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tif c \u003e= 'a' \u0026\u0026 c \u003c= 'f' {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tif c \u003e= 'A' \u0026\u0026 c \u003c= 'F' {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\treturn true\n}\n\nfunc (f *CommandFactory) resolveClosingCommitSHA(commitSHA string) (string, error) {\n\tnormalizedCommitSHA, err := f.resolveCommitSHA(commitSHA)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"invalid closing_commit_sha %q: %w\", commitSHA, err)\n\t}\n\tif normalizedCommitSHA == \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"\", only bead tracker state. Closing provenance is suppressed only for pure\n// tracker backfills that touch .ddx/beads.jsonl and nothing else.\nfunc (f *CommandFactory) commitIsMetadataOnlyTrackerBackfill(commitSHA string) bool {\n\tif commitSHA == \"\" {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)\n\tdefer cancel()\n\n\tcmd := gitpkg.Command(ctx, f.WorkingDir, \"show\", \"--pretty=format:\", \"--name-only\", commitSHA)\n\tout, err := cmd.Output()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, line := range strings.Split(string(out), \"\\n\") {\n\t\tpath := strings.TrimSpace(line)\n\t\tif path == \"\" {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tif path != \".ddx/beads.jsonl\" {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn true\n}\n\nfunc isReviewCloseBead(b *bead.Bead) bool {\n\tif b == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\tfor _, label := range b.Labels {\n\t\tswitch label {\n\t\tcase \"action:review\", \"kind:review\", \"phase:review\", \"review-finding\":\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\nfunc (f *CommandFactory) beadWorkspaceRoot() string {\n\tdir := os.Getenv(\"DDX_BEAD_DIR\")\n\tif dir != \"\" {\n\t\tif filepath.Base(dir) == \".ddx\" {\n\t\t\tif !filepath.IsAbs(dir) \u0026\u0026 f.WorkingDir != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tif workspaceRoot := gitpkg.FindNearestDDxWorkspace(f.WorkingDir); workspaceRoot != \"\" {\n\t\treturn workspaceRoot\n\t}\n\treturn f.WorkingDir\n}\n\nfunc (f *CommandFactory) beadStore() *bead.Store {\n\tworkspaceRoot := f.beadWorkspaceRoot()\n\tif workspaceRoot == \"\" {\n\t\treturn bead.NewStore(\"\")\n\t}\n\treturn bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(workspaceRoot, \".ddx\"))\n}\n\nfunc (f *CommandFactory) newBeadInitCommand() *cobra.Command {\n\treturn \u0026cobra.Command{\n\t\tUse:   \"init\",\n\t\tShort: \"Initialize bead storage\",\n\t\tArgs:  cobra.NoArgs,\n\t\tRunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {\n\t\t\ts := f.beadStore()\n\t\t\tif err := s.Init(); .ddx/executions/20260506T161425-8c0efb51/embedded/agent-codex-1778084072047707868.jsonl:10:{"type":"tool_result","sequence":9,"time":"2026-05-06T16:14:48.888486071Z","metadata":{"attempt_id":"20260506T161425-8c0efb51","base_rev":"0197b7c445024af743ca01eebad0ca1f30274f06","bead_id":"ddx-d0c656b0","bundle_path":".ddx/executions/20260506T161425-8c0efb51","prompt_file":".ddx/executions/20260506T161425-8c0efb51/prompt.md","prompt_sha":"047b36bdbe668fa6e487b15d64f81ec7ba763dc3c4a04350a69e5c2bc3be8a4c","session_id":"eb-f6b25d07","worker_id":""},"data":{"id":"item_5","output":"---\nddx:\n  id: FEAT-004\n  depends_on:\n    - helix.prd\n---\n# Feature: Beads (Work Item Tracker)\n\n**ID:** FEAT-004\n**Status:** Complete\n**Priority:** P0\n**Owner:** DDx Team\n\n## Overview\n\nBeads are portable, ephemeral work items with metadata. The `ddx bead` command provides CRUD, dependency tracking, and JSONL interchange — giving AI agents and workflows a structured work queue without coupling to any specific methodology.\n\nThe name follows the `bd` (Dolt-backed) and `br` (SQLite-backed) convention: short, lowercase tool names for the same conceptual unit. DDx beads use JSONL-backed local storage; `bd` and `br` provide database-backed alternatives. All three interchange via JSONL.\n\n## Problem Statement\n\n**Current situation:** AI agents need a work queue to know what to do next. HELIX built its own tracker (`scripts/tracker.sh`) tightly coupled to HELIX workflow semantics (phase labels, spec-id enforcement). Other workflows have no reusable work-tracking primitive.\n\n**Pain points:**\n- No generic work-item tool — every workflow reinvents issue tracking\n- HELIX tracker can't be used without HELIX's full label/validation requirements\n- No portable format for carrying work items between projects and tools\n- Agents can't discover ready work without workflow-specific logic\n\n**Desired outcome:** A lightweight, workflow-agnostic bead tracker that any methodology can layer semantics on top of. HELIX adds phase labels; a kanban workflow adds columns; a plain project just tracks tasks.\n\n## Requirements\n\n### Functional\n\n1. **Bead CRUD** (`ddx bead create/show/update/close`) — create, read, update, and close work items\n2. **Listing and filtering** (`ddx bead list`) — filter by status, label, or custom predicates\n3. **Dependency DAG** (`ddx bead dep add/remove/tree`) — declare ordering constraints between beads\n4. **Ready queue** (`ddx bead ready`) — list open beads with all dependencies satisfied\n5. **Blocked query** (`ddx bead blocked`) — list open beads with unsatisfied dependencies\n6. **Status summary** (`ddx bead status`) — counts of open, closed, blocked, ready beads\n7. **Import** (`ddx bead import`) — ingest beads from `bd`, `br`, or raw JSONL files\n8. **Export** (`ddx bead export`) — write beads as JSONL for interchange with other tools\n9. **Initialization** (`ddx bead init`) — create storage file and directory\n10. **Validation hooks** — workflows register custom validators for create/update operations\n11. **Claim ownership** (`ddx bead update \u003cid\u003e --claim [--assignee A]`) — claim a bead with explicit assignee control and stable claim metadata\n12. **Execution evidence** (`ddx bead evidence add/list`) — append-only history for close summaries, agent outputs, and experiment outcomes\n13. **Unknown field preservation** — round-trip fields DDx doesn't know about (enables workflow-specific extensions)\n14. **In-priority queue ordering override** (`ddx bead queue ...`) — let operators move a bead within its existing priority bucket without changing priority or extending the core bead schema\n\n### Non-Functional\n\n- **Performance:** All local operations complete in \u003c100ms for up to 10,000 beads. Read-heavy queue commands (`list`, `ready`, `blocked`, `status`) operate on one parsed snapshot and remain deterministic under repeated calls.\n- **Portability:** No external dependencies beyond the `ddx` binary\n- **Concurrency:** File-level locking prevents corruption from parallel writes. Writers use temp-file + atomic rename semantics, and repair flows keep a backup before swapping in a cleaned file.\n- **Recovery:** Malformed or partially written JSONL records are handled best-effort. Valid records are preserved, malformed lines are reported with line numbers, and a `.bak` backup is kept when the store auto-repairs a file.\n- **Interchange:** JSONL format compatible with `bd`, `br`, and HELIX tracker\n\n## Bead Schema\n\n| Field | Type | Required | Default | Notes |\n|-------|------|----------|---------|-------|\n| id | string | auto-generated | `bx-` + 8 hex chars | Prefix configurable |\n| title | string | yes | — | Non-empty |\n| type | string | no | `task` | Free-form (task, epic, bug, chore, etc.) |\n| status | string | no | `open` | Enum: open, in_progress, closed, blocked, proposed, cancelled (bd/br canonical set; see TD-031 §2) |\n| priority | int | no | 2 | Range 0-4 (0 = highest) |\n| labels | []string | no | [] | Free-form, no enforcement |\n| parent | string | no | \"\" | ID of parent bead |\n| description | string | no | \"\" | |\n| acceptance | string | no | \"\" | |\n| deps | []string | no | [] | IDs of dependency beads |\n| assignee | string | no | \"\" | |\n| notes | string | no | \"\" | |\n| created | datetime | auto | — | ISO-8601 UTC |\n| updated | datetime | auto | — | ISO-8601 UTC |\n\nUnknown fields in imported or existing beads are preserved on read/write. This allows HELIX to store `spec-id`, `execution-eligible`, `claimed-at`, `claimed-pid`, `superseded-by`, `replaces`, and DDx-specific queue metadata such as `queue-rank` without extending the core bd/br-compatible schema table.\n\nTD-031 defines how lifecycle actions use the existing carriers: persisted\nbd/br statuses, labels, dependency edges, append-only events, and preserved\n`Extra` fields. FEAT-004 does not introduce additional status vocabulary for\nno_changes, cooldown, superseded, or execution-readiness cases.\n\n### Queue Semantics For Epics\n\nEpics remain first-class beads, but they are not consumed by the same\nqueue-drain contract as ordinary executable task/bug/chore beads.\n\n- A normal execution-ready queue is **single-ticket-first**. Ready non-epic\n  beads are ordered ahead of ready epic beads at the same priority.\n- Open epics are not launched by the ordinary `ddx work` single-ticket worker\n  by default. They are consumed by an epic-scoped worker mode that owns an epic\n  branch and worktree.\n- Child beads of an epic remain individually executable units and may be\n  closed one-by-one as they land on the epic branch.\n- Epic queue entries remain visible in the tracker and UI, but their\n  execution semantics are governed by the epic worker contract rather than the\n  single-ticket loop contract.\n\nThis split preserves the simple `W2 = bead(W1)` contract for ordinary beads\nwhile allowing a separate sequential execution mode for epic branches.\n\n### Queue Ordering Overrides\n\nThe canonical ready-queue order is:\n\n1. `priority` ascending (`0` first, `4` last)\n2. explicit `queue-rank` ascending, with missing `queue-rank` sorted after\n   explicit ranks inside the same priority bucket\n3. `created_at` ascending\n4. `id` ascending\n\n`queue-rank` is preserved extension metadata stored in the bead's unknown-field\nmap, not a core bead schema field. It is an operator override for ordering\nwithin one priority bucket only. A ranked `P1` bead never sorts ahead of an\nunranked `P0` bead, and a ranked bead that is blocked, on retry cooldown,\nsuperseded, `execution-eligible=false`, or epic-only/container work remains\nexcluded from `ddx work`'s execution-ready picker.\n\nThe CLI exposes queue movement as a first-class surface rather than requiring\noperators to manage raw metadata:\n\n- `ddx bead queue top \u003cid\u003e` assigns a rank that places the bead first among\n  ready beads with the same priority.\n- `ddx bead queue move \u003cid\u003e --before \u003cother-id\u003e` places the bead before another\n  bead in the same priority bucket.\n- `ddx bead queue move \u003cid\u003e --after \u003cother-id\u003e` places the bead after another\n  bead in the same priority bucket.\n- `ddx bead queue clear \u003cid\u003e` removes the explicit rank and restores the\n  default tie-break ordering for that bead.\n\n`queue move --before/--after` fails when the two beads have different\npriorities. Operators that want to change urgency must use `ddx bead update\n\u003cid\u003e --priority N` explicitly. Queue-rank values are canonicalized as integers;\nread paths may accept numeric strings for compatibility, but writes persist a\nnumber. Rank calculation should use sparse integer values and renormalize only\nthe affected priority bucket when no midpoint exists.\n\n## Storage\n\nDDx beads abstracts over multiple storage backends. Consumers (HELIX, dun, other workflows) **must only interact via `ddx bead` commands or the server API** — never read/write the storage file directly.\n\n### Backend Selection\n\n| Backend | Config Value | When To Use |\n|---------|-------------|-------------|\n| `jsonl` (default) | `bead.backend: jsonl` | Simple projects, single-agent, no external tooling |\n| `bd` | `bead.backend: bd` | Dolt-backed bead store (collaborative, branching) |\n| `br` | `bead.backend: br` | SQLite-backed bead store (local performance) |\n\nBackend is configured in `.ddx/config.yaml` or via `DDX_BEAD_BACKEND` env var. Default: `jsonl`.\n\nFor `bd` and `br` backends, DDx shells out to the respective binary. For `jsonl`, DDx manages the file directly.\n\n### JSONL Backend Details\n\n- **Default path:** `.ddx/beads.jsonl` (one JSON object per line, sorted by id)\n- **Configuration:** `DDX_BEAD_DIR` env var or `bead.dir` in `.ddx/config.yaml` (default: `.ddx`)\n- **Locking:** Directory-based lock at `.ddx/beads.lock/` with PID file and acquisition timestamp. Configurable timeout (default: 10s).\n- **Write algorithm:** Mutating operations take the lock, build a complete bead snapshot, write to `beads.jsonl.tmp`, and atomically rename the temp file into place. This avoids partially written tracker state.\n- **Read algorithm:** Queue commands scan `beads.jsonl` line-by-line. Valid JSON objects are loaded into a snapshot; malformed lines are skipped with line-numbered warnings so one bad record does not take down the entire queue.\n- **Repair algorithm:** If a read finds malformed lines but at least one valid bead, DDx takes the store lock, copies the current file to `.ddx/beads.jsonl.bak`, and rewrites the repaired snapshot atomically.\n- **Failure mode:** If every line is malformed, the command returns a contextual error that names the file and malformed-record count.\n- **Git integration:** `beads.jsonl` is project state and must be committed to git. Auto-commit behavior after bead mutations is defined in FEAT-012 (Git Awareness), requirements S7 and US-124. `ddx init` generates agent guidance that includes `beads.jsonl` as a committable file (FEAT-012 S8, US-125).\n- **Design reference:** See [`SD-004-beads-tracker.md`](../../02-design/solution-designs/SD-004-beads-tracker.md), [`TD-004-beads-claims-evidence.md`](../../02-design/technical-designs/TD-004-beads-claims-evidence.md), and [`TP-004-beads-claims-evidence.md`](../../03-test/test-plans/TP-004-beads-claims-evidence.md) for the concrete algorithms, repair flow, claim semantics, evidence trail, and validation matrix.\n\n### Storage Boundary\n\nThe bead backend is the reusable DDx storage engine for bead-schema records.\nThe primary work queue is one collection, not the only possible collection.\n\n- The default active-work collection maps to `beads.jsonl` in the JSONL backend.\n- Other DDx services may use separate bead-backed collections for archived\n  work, execution history, agent session indexes, or other record families.\n- Large payloads such as prompt bodies or raw logs live in separate attachment\n  files referenced from the bead-schema record rather than inline in the\n  primary collection row.\n- Backend selection is a store-level concern, not a single-file concern.\n\n### ID Prefix\n\nFollowing the bd convention, the prefix is **derived from the repository name** by default:\n\n- **Auto-detected:** git repo root directory name (e.g., repo `my-project` → prefix `my-project` → IDs like `my-project-a3f2dd`)\n- **Override:** `bead.id_prefix` in `.ddx/config.yaml` or `DDX_BEAD_PREFIX` env var\n- **Fallback:** `bx` if repo name detection fails\n- **Format:** `\u003cprefix\u003e-` + 8 hex chars\n\nThis matches how bd derives its prefix, ensuring beads created by DDx and bd in the same repo use the same naming convention.\n\n## Validation\n\n**DDx base validation** (always applied):\n- Title is non-empty\n- Dependencies reference existing bead IDs\n- No self-referential dependencies\n- Priority is in valid range (0-4)\n- Status is a recognized value\n\n**Workflow validation hooks:** An executable at `.ddx/hooks/validate-bead-create` (and `validate-bead-update`) receives the bead JSON on stdin. Exit codes: 0 = ok, 1 = hard error (stderr = message, creation blocked), 2 = warning (stderr = message, creation proceeds).\n\n### Authoring quality lint\n\nADR-023 adds lifecycle-quality lint on top of the existing validation-hook\nsurface. Base validation still protects the reusable bead schema; authoring\nquality lint protects the \"bead as prompt\" contract needed by `ddx try`,\n`ddx work`, and autonomous sub-agent execution.\n\nThe lint rubric is the 8-criterion template in\n`docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md`:\n\n- title is scoped and imperative\n- description contains problem, root cause with file:line, proposed fix, and\n  non-scope\n- acceptance criteria are numbered, verifiable, and name concrete tests or\n  observable artifacts when tests apply\n- new code paths include wired-in assertions\n- acceptance criteria name the applicable `go test` command and\n  `lefthook run pre-commit`\n"}}

The lease is held until the process is killed. Observed and MANUALLY SALVAGED ~5x on 2026-05-27 (beads ddx-606fe854, ddx-dc23f001, ddx-9714eaac, ddx-58a69bb0; longest single wedge 97 min). This is the top blocker to unattended `ddx work` drain.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE\u0026#xA;- cli/internal/agent/work/watchdog.go phaseEmpty(harness, model, route) returns true only when ALL THREE of harness, model, route are empty.\u0026#xA;- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2177 liveness.SetAttempt(candidate.ID, provAttemptID, PhaseRunning, profile, harness, model, profile, 0) sets harness (non-empty) once at attempt start and NEVER passes route; no later call updates model/route on the liveness record.\u0026#xA;- Therefore once an attempt is running, harness != \u0026#34;\u0026#34; so phaseEmpty() is permanently false, and RunProgressWatchdog (watchdog.go:102, wired at execute_bead_loop.go:2210) can NEVER fire for the running/post-commit phase it was designed to catch (ddx-8f2e0ebf criterion B). The route-resolution timeout (ddx-d8970a7b) only bounds the PRE-route window. So the post-commit review+land hang is unguarded by both mechanisms.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;PROPOSED FIX (recommended: bound the post-commit phase, do not rely on field-emptiness)\u0026#xA;Add a configurable timeout around the post-commit review+land orchestration in the attempt (mirroring the route-resolution timeout d8970a7b): when the implementation subprocess has returned/committed and the worker is in review or land, bound that work with a deadline (default e.g. 15m). On expiry: cancel the attempt context, release the lease, mark the attempt failed/preserved, and emit an operator-attention event (reuse flagWedgedForOperatorAttention). Field-emptiness (phaseEmpty) is the WRONG discriminator for a running attempt that has already done real work, so the fix must be progress/elapsed-based, not field-based.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;NON-SCOPE\u0026#xA;- Do not change the route-resolution timeout (ddx-d8970a7b) or the external-close watcher (ddx-dc23f001 RunExternalCloseWatcher).\u0026#xA;- Do not change the resolving-phase all-empty detection (still valid before harness is assigned).\u0026#xA;- Do not change harness/model routing.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;CROSS-REF: follow-up to closed epic ddx-8f2e0ebf (criterion B was not actually covered by the landed progress watchdog).     \u003c/description\u003e     \u003cacceptance\u003e 1. The post-commit review+land orchestration is bounded by a configurable timeout (default documented); on expiry the attempt context is cancelled, the lease is released, and an operator-attention event is emitted. Verified by an integration test (e.g. TestExecuteBead_PostCommitReviewLandTimeoutReleasesLease in cli/internal/agent) that injects a hanging review/land stub and asserts the lease is released within timeout+epsilon with an operator-attention record.\u0026#xA;2. A review+land that completes within the budget is unaffected (regression): TestExecuteBead_PostCommitReviewLandWithinBudgetCompletes (or equivalent) passes.\u0026#xA;3. The fix is elapsed/progress-based, not phaseEmpty-based: a running attempt with harness set and model/route empty that exceeds the budget is released (asserted by the test in AC1), proving the watchdog gap is closed.\u0026#xA;4. cd cli \u0026amp;\u0026amp; go test ./internal/agent/... green.\u0026#xA;5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.     \u003c/acceptance\u003e     \u003clabels\u003earea:execute-bead, area:workers, kind:bug, kind:reliability, priority:reliability, severity:high, observability, spec:FEAT-010, prevention:ddx-8f2e0ebf\u003c/labels\u003e     \u003cmetadata base-rev=\"18690e8f4da01c08142eb41adabb18b8f087223a\" bundle=\".ddx/executions/20260528T030201-c5a41e62\"/\u003e   \u003c/bead\u003e   \u003cgoverning\u003e     \u003cnote\u003eNo governing references were pre-resolved. .ddx/executions/20260607T004106-90614c51/prompt.md:3:You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add` only for legitimate child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges; never the parent.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Don't mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; don't parallelize `git add`, `git commit`, or staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; subject ends with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add` for child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.

.ddx/executions/20260527T010457-95a673d9/prompt.md:3:You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Do not mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; do not use parallel tool calls for `git add`, `git commit`, or other staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 still allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add`, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

Fizeau\u0026#39;s `default` profile (`fizeau/internal/modelcatalog/catalog/models.yaml:438-443`) hard-pins `min_power: 7, max_power: 8`. For operators whose model catalog has no power-7-or-8 entries — e.g., local providers (power 5–6) plus frontier cloud (power 9) — `routePowerPolicyForRequest` overlays those bounds and the routing engine rejects every candidate as `below_min_power` or `above_max_power`. Result: zero-config `ddx work` claims a bead, dispatches with empty intent, fizeau returns \u0026#34;no viable routing candidate,\u0026#34; and the agent exits in \u0026lt;1s with `no_evidence_produced`. Power was meant to be a hint, not a gate; synthesizing a constraint the operator never asked for inverts that policy. Repro: in this repo with no flags, `ddx work --once` triggers the dispatch-failure on hooks today and would trigger it on the implementation dispatch too if explicit `--harness/--model` flags weren\u0026#39;t bypassing the bound via fizeau\u0026#39;s exact-pin escape (`routePowerBoundsForRequest`).\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE\u0026#xA;- `cli/internal/agent/routing_profile.go:9-15` returns the constant `\u0026#34;default\u0026#34;` whenever the operator-supplied profile is empty.\u0026#xA;- `cli/cmd/work.go:70` registers the `--profile` flag with default value `agent.DefaultRoutingProfile`, so cobra fills in `\u0026#34;default\u0026#34;` even when the operator omits the flag.\u0026#xA;- `cli/cmd/try.go:86` does the same for `ddx try`.\u0026#xA;- `cli/cmd/run.go:137`, `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:298`, `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1613`, `cli/cmd/try.go:215`, and `cli/internal/server/workers.go:{303,409,705}` all call `NormalizeRoutingProfile`, inheriting the synthesis.\u0026#xA;- Downstream fizeau gating: `routePowerPolicyForRequest` (`fizeau/service_routing.go:1117-1141`) returns zero bounds only when `req.Profile == \u0026#34;\u0026#34;`; any non-empty profile string causes a catalog lookup that overlays its `min_power`/`max_power` onto the request.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;PROPOSED FIX\u0026#xA;- Change `NormalizeRoutingProfile` to return the trimmed input as-is; an empty operator value stays empty. Keep the function (callers want trim+normalize), drop the synthesis branch.\u0026#xA;- Remove the `DefaultRoutingProfile = \u0026#34;default\u0026#34;` constant, or repurpose it strictly for the help-text label rather than a runtime default. Update `cli/cmd/work.go:70` and `cli/cmd/try.go:86` so the cobra flag\u0026#39;s default is `\u0026#34;\u0026#34;` and the help string reads \u0026#34;Routing profile: cheap, fast, smart (empty = unconstrained, let the agent service choose)\u0026#34;.\u0026#xA;- Audit every caller of `NormalizeRoutingProfile` to confirm they tolerate `\u0026#34;\u0026#34;` (today they all forward the value into `CLIOverrides.Profile` or a workers spec — empty is already valid downstream).\u0026#xA;- After this change, zero-config `ddx work` sends `Profile=\u0026#34;\u0026#34;` → fizeau applies no profile-derived power bound → routing picks any available model regardless of power tier, honoring the \u0026#34;power is a hint\u0026#34; policy.\u0026#xA;- The pre-dispatch lint and post-attempt triage hooks (`lint_hook.go`, `triage_hook.go`) still need `ClearProfile: true` so they ignore an explicitly operator-set `--profile fast`/`--profile standard` (those profiles also pin power 7-8); that defensive change is tracked in ddx-c54b817c and remains correct.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;NON-SCOPE\u0026#xA;- Do not change fizeau\u0026#39;s `default` profile or its power-gating semantics; making power bounds advisory rather than hard is a fizeau-side change and out of scope here.\u0026#xA;- Do not change `--min-power`/`--max-power` flag handling. When the operator explicitly asks for a power bound, honor it; this bead only stops DDx from inventing one.\u0026#xA;- Do not remove `cheap`/`fast`/`smart`/`standard` profile values — they remain available as explicit operator opt-ins.\u0026#xA;- Do not modify ddx-c54b817c\u0026#39;s hook-level fix; it is independently defensive against operator-set profiles.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;PARENT + DEPS\u0026#xA;No parent. agent\n\n// execute_bead_land.go — the land coordinator pattern.\n//\n// This file implements the human-PR landing model for execute-bead results.\n// The old Merge() path in execute_bead_orchestrator.go has been deleted; all\n// target-ref writes now flow through Land(), which is called exactly once per\n// submission by a per-project coordinator goroutine (see\n// internal/server/workers.go:LandCoordinator).\n//\n// The flow mirrors how a human merges PRs:\n//\n//   1. Read the current target tip from the local branch.\n//   2. If the current tip equals the worker's BaseRev — fast-forward the\n//      target branch directly to the worker's ResultRev via update-ref. The\n//      worker's commit keeps its original parent; no new commit is created.\n//   3. Otherwise — the target has advanced since the worker started. Create\n//      a temporary detached worktree at the current target tip, run\n//      `git merge --no-ff` to introduce the worker's ResultRev, and\n//      fast-forward the target branch to the resulting merge commit. The\n//      worker's commit is NEVER rewritten: its parent is still BaseRev, so\n//      a later replay observes the same inputs the worker saw.\n//   4. If the merge conflicts — abort cleanly, preserve the original\n//      ResultRev under refs/ddx/iterations/\u003cbead-id\u003e/\u003cattempt-id\u003e-\u003cshort-tip\u003e,\n//      and return preserved status. Target branch is never modified.\n//   5. If evidence needs to be folded in, stage and commit it locally, then\n//      sync the operator checkout to the new head when safe to do so.\n//\n// Network sync is intentionally out of scope for Land(): no fetch and no\n// push happen here. Operators sync origin with raw git commands after the\n// drain session completes.\n//\n// Replay fidelity is the reason for merge-over-rebase: a rebased commit has\n// a rewritten parent that lies about what the worker saw at execution time.\n// A merge commit preserves both histories — the worker's original commit\n// (parent = BaseRev) and the target's prior tip — losslessly.\n//\n// The in-process coordinator serializes submissions inside one process. Land()\n// also takes the process-shared main-git lock so separate `ddx work` processes\n// Land narrows the process-shared main-git lock to the shared-ref update\n// boundary so separate `ddx work` processes cannot interleave target-ref\n// writes while read-only prep stays outside the lock and post-land checkout\n// sync runs under the same lock acquisition.\n\nimport (\n\t\"bytes\"\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"encoding/json\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"go/parser\"\n\t\"go/token\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"os/exec\"\n\t\"path/filepath\"\n\t\"strconv\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config\"\n\tinternalgit \"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/git\"\n\t\"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/trackerpaths\"\n)\n\nconst defaultLargeDeletionLineThreshold = 200\n\n// LandRequest is one submission to the land coordinator: \"here is the worker's\n// result from base B to result R for bead X; land it on the project's target\n// branch.\"\ntype LandRequest struct {\n\t// WorktreeDir is the path to the project's repository directory (the\n\t// directory the coordinator operates on). The original worker worktree\n\t// has typically already been removed by the time Land() runs — Land()\n\t// creates its own temporary worktrees when a merge is needed.\n\tWorktreeDir string\n\n\t// BaseRev is the revision the worker branched off when it started.\n\t// When the current target tip equals BaseRev, Land() takes the fast path.\n\tBaseRev string\n\n\t// ResultRev is the worker's final commit SHA. Must be reachable in the\n\t// project's git object store at the time Land() is called.\n\tResultRev string\n\n\t// BeadID identifies the bead this submission is for. Used to build the\n\t// preserve-ref path on conflict.\n\tBeadID string\n\n\t// AttemptID uniquely identifies this land attempt. Used to build the\n\t// preserve-ref path on conflict so concurrent attempts for the same\n\t// bead do not collide.\n\tAttemptID string\n\n\t// TargetBranch is the branch to advance. When empty, Land() resolves\n\t// the project's current HEAD branch and uses that.\n\tTargetBranch string\n\n\t// EvidenceDir is the relative path to the per-attempt execution evidence\n\t// directory (e.g. \".ddx/executions/20260416T181205-b5419982\"). When\n\t// non-empty and the main land succeeds, Land() creates a trailing\n\t// evidence commit that folds these files into the target branch. The\n\t// agent's commit SHA is NOT amended — the evidence commit is a separate\n\t// child commit, preserving closing_commit_sha references.\n\tEvidenceDir string\n\n\t// PostLandCommand is an optional project verification command run after\n\t// Land() advances the local target ref and syncs the worktree, but before\n\t// evidence commit creation or checkout sync. A failure restores the target\n\t// ref to its pre-land SHA and preserves ResultRev under refs/ddx/iterations.\n\tPostLandCommand []string\n\n\t// LargeDeletionLineThreshold overrides the default per-file deletion gate\n\t// threshold. Zero or negative uses defaultLargeDeletionLineThreshold.\n\tLargeDeletionLineThreshold int\n}\n\n// LandResult describes the outcome of a single Land() call.\ntype LandResult struct {\n\t// Status is one of:\n\t//   - \"landed\":    the target branch now points at a new commit\n\t//                  (either ResultRev itself on the fast-forward path,\n\t//                  or a merge commit on the merge path).\n\t//   - \"preserved\": the merge conflicted; ResultRev is saved under\n\t//                  PreserveRef and the target branch is unchanged.\n\t//   - \"no-changes\": ResultRev == BaseRev; nothing to land.\n\tStatus string\n\n\t// NewTip is the new value of the target branch when Status == \"landed\".\n\t// On the fast-forward path NewTip == ResultRev; on the merge path NewTip\n\t// is the SHA of the merge commit (whose parents are the prior target\n\t// tip and ResultRev). Empty when preserved or no-changes.\n\tNewTip string\n\n\t// LandedTip is the target branch tip immediately after the implementation\n\t// (or already-committed evidence bundle) lands, but before any trailing\n\t// audit-only evidence/final-result commit. When no trailing commit is\n\t// created it matches NewTip.\n\tLandedTip string\n\n\t// TargetBranch is the resolved branch name that Land() advanced or attempted\n\t// to advance. It is set on landed and preserved results so callers can make\n\t// branch-local recovery explicit in terminal output and evidence.\n\tTargetBranch string\n\n\t// Merged is true when the land took the merge-commit path (current tip\n\t// had advanced beyond BaseRev, so Land() created a merge commit to\n\t// combine the worker's result with the new target tip). False on the\n\t// fast-forward path where the worker's commit became the new tip\n\t// unchanged.\n\tMerged bool\n\n\t// PreserveRef is set when Status == \"preserved\". It names the ref under\n\t// refs/ddx/iterations/ where ResultRev was saved for later recovery.\n\tPreserveRef string\n\n\t// Reason is a human-readable explanation, especially useful when\n\t// Status == \"preserved\" (e.g. \"merge conflict\").\n\tReason string\n\n\t// MergedCommitCount is the number of commits reachable from ResultRev but\n\t// not from BaseRev — i.e. the \"size\" of the worker's contribution being\n\t// merged in. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;## Review gate\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.\u0026#xA;- Address every BLOCKING `\u0026lt;review-findings\u0026gt;` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;## Constraints\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;- Work only inside this execution worktree.\u0026#xA;- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.\u0026#xA;- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.\u0026#xA;- Do not modify files outside the bead\u0026#39;s named scope.\u0026#xA;- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation \u0026gt; 60s per variant):\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.\u0026#xA;- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.\u0026#xA;- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;## When the work is done\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.   \u003c/instructions\u003e   \u003cbead id=\"ddx-5e8dac57\"\u003e     \u003ctitle\u003eagent: enforce the tracker-lock hold cap with a force-release watchdog (InstrumentCapped)\u003c/title\u003e     \u003cdescription\u003e PROBLEM: A 4m37s land_post_land_finalize lock hold (9.2x the 30s TP-021 tracker cap) occurred with NO watchdog firing — the cap is recorded but never enforced, so a runaway hold blocks the sibling worker indefinitely.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;ROOT CAUSE: cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:287 instruments the lock with lockmetrics.Instrument(\u0026#34;tracker.lock\u0026#34;, op, fn) (METRICS ONLY). The cap-enforcing lockmetrics.InstrumentCapped (cli/internal/lockmetrics/lockcap.go:93) with DefaultTrackerLockCap=30s (lockcap.go:23) is never wired into withTrackerLockPolicy (tracker_lock.go:215). So the cap is observed but not enforced — no force-release watchdog.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;PROPOSED FIX: Wire withTrackerLockPolicy through lockmetrics.InstrumentCapped with CapConfig{Cap: lockmetrics.CapFor(\u0026#34;tracker.lock\u0026#34;)} so a hold past the cap fires the watchdog (stack capture, force-release, lock-violation.json evidence). Keep existing Instrument metrics behavior. This is a safety backstop; the land beads (L1/L2) remove the legitimate slow holds so the watchdog should not fire in normal operation.\u0026#xA;\u0026#xA;NON-SCOPE: removing the slow ops (L1/L2 do that); changing the cap value.     \u003c/description\u003e     \u003cacceptance\u003e 1. New TestTrackerLock_HoldPastCapForceReleasesAndRecordsViolation (tracker_lock_test.go): hold the lock past an overridden small cap (DDX_LOCK_CAP_TRACKER_MS); assert force-release fires and a lock-violation is recorded.\u0026#xA;2. Existing cli/internal/lockmetrics/lockcap_test.go and tracker_lock_test.go (incl TestTrackerLock_SectionField) pass.\u0026#xA;3. cd cli \u0026amp;\u0026amp; go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/lockmetrics/... -run \u0026#39;TestTrackerLock_HoldPastCapForceReleasesAndRecordsViolation|TestTrackerLock_SectionField|TestCap\u0026#39; -count=1 green.\u0026#xA;4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.     \u003c/acceptance\u003e     \u003clabels\u003earea:agent, area:git, kind:bug, kind:reliability, phase:iterate, spec:TP-021, prevention:ddx-6650fdc9, incident:land-lock-hold\u003c/labels\u003e     \u003cmetadata parent=\"ddx-3106674f\" base-rev=\"da5ca63fe8a6a5c3000ba6ba60732e8be134f570\" bundle=\".ddx/executions/20260607T004106-90614c51\"/\u003e   \u003c/bead\u003e   \u003cgoverning\u003e     \u003cnote\u003eNo governing references were pre-resolved. data.text: I’ve narrowed this to the tracker command layer and the bead store validation path. Next I’m opening the specific create/dep-add code and the existing tests around parent/dependency handling so I can patch the exact guard instead of guessing at the plumbing.

There’s already partial coverage in the tree, which is useful: I’m checking whether the current implementation only rejects the direct parent or already walks ancestors. If it’s partial, I’ll tighten the guard and align the error text across `dep add` and `create` so the two paths behave identically.

data.output:
package cmd

import (
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestBeadCreateRejectsDependsOnParentAncestor(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	rootOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Root", "--type", "task")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	rootID := strings.TrimSpace(rootOut)
	require.NotEmpty(t, rootID)

	parentOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Parent", "--type", "task", "--parent", rootID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	parentID := strings.TrimSpace(parentOut)
	require.NotEmpty(t, parentID)

	t.Run("direct-parent", func(t *testing.T) {
		_, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Child direct", "--type", "task", "--parent", parentID, "--depends-on", parentID)
		require.Error(t, err)
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "ancestor in the parent chain")
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), parentID)
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), parentID+" -> "+rootID)
	})

	t.Run("grandparent-ancestor", func(t *testing.T) {
		_, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Child ancestor", "--type", "task", "--parent", parentID, "--depends-on", rootID)
		require.Error(t, err)
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "ancestor in the parent chain")
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), rootID)
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), parentID+" -> "+rootID)
	})
}

package cmd

import (
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestBeadCreateRejectsDependsOnParentAncestor(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	rootOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Root", "--type", "task")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	rootID := strings.TrimSpace(rootOut)
	require.NotEmpty(t, rootID)

	parentOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Parent", "--type", "task", "--parent", rootID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	parentID := strings.TrimSpace(parentOut)
	require.NotEmpty(t, parentID)

	t.Run("direct-parent", func(t *testing.T) {
		_, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Child direct", "--type", "task", "--parent", parentID, "--depends-on", parentID)
		require.Error(t, err)
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "ancestor in the parent chain")
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), parentID)
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), parentID+" -> "+rootID)
	})

	t.Run("grandparent-ancestor", func(t *testing.T) {
		_, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Child ancestor", "--type", "task", "--parent", parentID, "--depends-on", rootID)
		require.Error(t, err)
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "ancestor in the parent chain")
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), rootID)
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), parentID+" -> "+rootID)
	})
}

The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add` only for legitimate child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges; never the parent.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Don't mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; don't parallelize `git add`, `git commit`, or staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; subject ends with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add` for child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.   </instructions>   <bead id="ddx-f9bb7327">     <title>Detect and repair parent-ancestor deps in bead doctor</title>     <description> PROBLEM
`ddx bead doctor` can currently repair oversized fields and detect dangling-success beads, but it never scans for dependency edges that point back to a bead's parent chain. That leaves structural back-edges hidden until work loops wedge.

ROOT CAUSE
`cli/internal/bead/doctor.go` only reports field-length overflow, and `cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go` only exposes the oversized-field and dangling-success modes. There is no stable, scriptable `parent_ancestor_in_deps` pass that enumerates offending beads or repairs them under `--fix`.

PROPOSED FIX
Extend the bead doctor to scan for any dep whose target is the bead's parent or ancestor, emit a stable `parent_ancestor_in_deps` finding list, and remove only the offending edges when `--fix` is supplied. The command output must stay deterministic so shell tooling can parse it. Add a regression test named `ddx_doctor_detects_and_repairs_back_edge`.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change create-time validation, decomposition prompt text, work-loop remediation, or dispatch severity in this bead.

In-scope files:
- cli/internal/bead/doctor.go
- cli/internal/bead/doctor_test.go
- cli/cmd/bead_doctor.go
- cli/cmd/bead_doctor_test.go (new or existing command-level coverage)

Out-of-scope:
- cli/internal/bead/store.go
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
- cli/internal/agent/lifecycle_dispatch.go
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md     </description>     <acceptance> 1. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... -run TestBeadDoctorDetectsAndRepairsBackEdge` passes.
2. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... -run TestBeadDoctorDetectsAndRepairsBackEdge` passes.
3. `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/...` passes.
4. `cd cli && lefthook run pre-commit` passes.     </acceptance>     <labels>area:tracker, area:lifecycle, area:decomposition, kind:safety, kind:correctness, ac-quality:needs-refinement</labels>     <metadata parent="ddx-e19185aa" base-rev="63f23cd73fa79391b7127fd0bb918f7cd6ba4998" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260607T014322-2c9bfd95"/>   </bead>   <governing>     <note>No governing references were pre-resolved.
